news_summary: Oman signed a mineral exploration agreement with the UK’s Knights Bay to explore for and extract nickel, described as the first deal of its kind with a foreign partner. Initial investment for exploration and evaluation is estimated at $25–$30 million over the first three years, with no near-term output details. | EV Nickel Inc. announces upcoming presentations at PDAC and other events and says it is awaiting assay results from its 2023 W4 drilling later in March. It also discusses clean nickel strategy and a previously released CarLang A Zone maiden resource estimate. No new production or pricing data is provided. | Oman’s Ministry of Energy and Minerals signed a mining concession with Knights Bay for Block 21, giving the UK firm rights to extract high-purity nickel with zero carbon emissions. The project includes exploration and appraisal and plans to process battery minerals (nickel and cobalt) for EV industry demand. | Sky Gold Corp. says the TSX Venture Exchange accepted its 100% acquisition of three nickel-copper-cobalt-platinum group element properties in Ontario. The deal terms include share issuance, cash, and a net smelter/royalty. No production or development timeline is provided, implying limited short-term supply impact for nickel and copper. | Sky Gold Corp. reports that TSX-V has accepted filing for Sky’s 100% acquisition of three nickel-copper-cobalt-PGE properties in northwestern Ontario. The deal terms involve share issuance and cash payments, plus potential additional consideration if a large, viable ore body is identified—no near-term production impact is stated.

London copper was set for a weekly gain as stronger-than-expected China manufacturing and services data boosted demand sentiment, while declining SHFE inventories suggested improving tightness. Markets also weighed a firmer dollar and potential additional U.S. sanctions on China. Aluminium rose slightly; nickel fell on the day. | SMM says bonded zone nickel inventory fell 1,000 mt to 5,600 mt week-on-week as of March 3. Nickel plate stock dropped to 4,130 mt, while briquettes were flat at 1,470 mt due to low stainless steel demand. Spot trading picked up slightly amid lower nickel prices. | The UK Financial Conduct Authority launched an enforcement investigation into the London Metal Exchange’s nickel trading halt decision and its systems and controls around Jan. 1 to March 8, 2022. The regulator appears focused on stopping trade, not on canceling trades. Bank of England reviews also flagged LME Clear shortcomings. | Three Chinese migrant workers filed a human-rights complaint alleging abuse and unsafe conditions at a nickel processing estate in Indonesia, including alleged forced overtime, lack of protective equipment, and withholding passports. The company denied involvement with the IMIP Morowali complex. Allegations could heighten scrutiny and disrupt nickel operations.

SMM reports Ni 1.5% ore CIF prices at about $72/wmt, steady week-on-week, with low Philippines shipments. Supply tightness is pushing mines to seek higher ore quotes, but NPI demand looks weak: traders restocked earlier, hedgers may lower prices, and NPI plants may reject higher ore costs. SMM expects further NPI declines. | SMM says Chinese nickel ore inventories fell 172,000 wmt week-on-week to 7.0 million wmt (55,000 mt Ni content) by March 3, citing low recent arrivals and rigid NPI purchasing. It expects inventories to keep trending lower, potentially around 6 million before the end of the Philippines rainy season. | Nickel ore arrivals at Chinese ports totaled 498,000 wmt as of Feb 24, up 27% week-on-week, with shipments landing at several major ports. The ore is mainly for delivery to NPI plants in Shandong, Jiangsu and Liaoning. Overall imports remain low and downstream demand is rigid, limiting stock build-up. | SMM reports pricing pressure on NORNICKEL nickel affecting nickel sulphate spreads and near-zero electrowinning profits. Ternary demand is weak and firms prefer cheaper liquid nickel sulphate, while nickel sulphate supply is tight due to delayed Indonesia raw-material line commissioning and late February restarts. Costs support prices; expected range is 39,000–40,000 yuan/mt this week. | SMM reports nickel sulphate enterprises’ margins fell further as demand rose but new MHP capacity commissioning was delayed. Nickel salt plants faced high-priced raw materials and tighter high-grade nickel matte supply (some matte lines switched to NPI). Lower downstream purchase prices pushed firms toward reduced profits or losses. | The UK FCA has opened an enforcement investigation into the LME’s 2022 suspension of nickel trading, examining conduct and controls around 1 Jan to 8 Mar 2022 after a surge in nickel prices linked to Russia-Ukraine supply concerns. The Bank of England also flagged governance and risk-management shortcomings at LME Clear. | Electric Royalties announced it will acquire a 0.5% gross revenue royalty (with options for additional royalty) on the Kenbridge nickel project in Ontario. The project is described as nickel-copper mineralized, with a preliminary economic assessment using assumed Ni and Cu prices, but no near-term production changes are stated. | The UK FCA has opened an investigation into the LME’s March 2022 suspension of nickel futures, reviewing trading activity from early 2022 until the March 8 halt. The suspension followed a brief nickel surge above $100,000/t. FCA highlights transparency and risk management as LME may adjust market structure. | The UK FCA announced an enforcement investigation into some of the LME’s conduct and controls between Jan. 1, 2022 and the nickel contract suspension on March 8, 2022. The article notes the probe relates to the period of disorderly nickel trading and adds ongoing uncertainty for LME nickel confidence. | EV Nickel Inc. announced it received CAD 500,000 from the Ontario government for its “Clean Nickel” strategy, including R&D on bioleaching and carbon capture to produce lower-carbon nickel from its Shaw Dome project near Timmins. The non-dilutive funding supports innovation for EV battery supply. | Canada Nickel reported assay results from the first 12 holes at its Texmont nickel project, finding near-surface, thick, high-grade mineralization with multiple intervals above 1% nickel and remaining open at depth and to the north. Management highlighted potential near-term, smaller open-pit production, supporting bullish sentiment for nickel supply prospects.

Hedge funds are suing the London Metal Exchange over the cancellation of billions of dollars of nickel trades after the exchange suspended nickel in March following a violent price spike. The FCA is investigating possible misconduct, while other lawsuits are ongoing, increasing nickel market uncertainty. | Goldman Sachs Commodities Research said lithium prices are expected to fall further and will move lower alongside cobalt and nickel. The note projects a downtrend over the next nine to 12 months, implying bearish sentiment for nickel prices, though the timing extends beyond the immediate weeks focus. | Copper prices edged higher as the dollar weakened and traders anticipated improving China demand from March. LME three-month copper rose slightly, and Shanghai April copper also increased. Aluminium eased on the day and nickel declined, indicating the bullish impulse was strongest for copper rather than industrial metals broadly. | PT Aneka Tambang reported improved unaudited January 2023 performance for its nickel business. Ferronickel production rose to 2,113 tons (+28% YoY) and sales to 2,013 TNi. Nickel ore production reached 1.06 million wmt (+16% YoY) with sales at 1.08 million wmt (+80% YoY). | LME data shows nickel inventories dropping to a multi-year low around 43,884 mt before a slight rebound, then easing again to 44,628 mt. SHFE inventories also widened but rose 14.72% last week to 2,884 mt, indicating tighter LME visibility while domestic stocks recovered.

FPX Nickel reportedly received funding from the Government of Canada aimed at scaling up production of nickel and cobalt for EV battery supply chains. Additional support for output expansion can shift expectations for nickel availability and demand dynamics, potentially supporting nickel prices over the next few weeks. | Harita Nickel begins a $600m investor roadshow ahead of a public listing, in a broader Indonesian push to retain nickel processing after the 2019 raw nickel export ban. The country’s EV-battery investments and growing nickel export value support nickel demand/supply expectations, with some peripheral copper IPO plans mentioned. | Aurubis is constructing the Bleed Treatment Olen Beerse (BOB) hydrometallurgical facility to recover copper and nickel from electrolytes/“bleed” streams at its recycling sites, boosting recycling capacity. Production is scheduled for late 2024 with full operations in 2025/26, so near-term price impact is limited despite involvement of both metals. | Aurubis is building the Bleed treatment Olen Beerse (BOB) hydrometallurgical plant to process copper electrolysis bleed and recover metals including copper and nickel. The facility targets higher recovery from electrolyte streams, with commissioning planned for 2H 2024 and full operation in 2025/26, supporting supply expectations.

SMM reported bonded zone nickel inventory fell 500 mt to 5,100 mt as of March 10. Nickel plate stock declined more amid weak downstream demand, reflecting dropping nickel prices. Nickel briquette demand from stainless steel stayed low, leaving briquette inventories broadly flat. Overall, the inventory move aligns with modest expectations. | Canada announced a ban on importing Russian steel and aluminum as part of sanctions over the Ukraine invasion. Reported additional curtailed trade is expected to be small because Russian shipments had already declined and imports faced prior tariffs, though the move may add some supply-chain pressure. Copper and nickel are not addressed. | Canada will ban imports of Russian steel and aluminum under its sanctions against Moscow. Foreign Affairs Minister Joly ties the policy to weakening Russia’s ability to attack Ukraine and possible regime change. Industry sources say Russia’s Canadian aluminum/steel volumes are small and the overall commercial impact is likely muted, limiting price effects.

Indonesian villagers on Wawonii Island protest nickel mining expansion, alleging threats to land rights and environmental damage. Farmers report land seizures, clashes, detentions, and attacks on or obstruction of mining equipment. Indonesia’s growing nickel processing build-out increases supply risk in the near term, potentially tightening expectations. | Indonesian farmers in Sulawesi are fighting nickel miners over land rights, with reports of detentions, protests, and equipment set on fire. Indonesia’s nickel boom involves expanding mines and processing plants, but ongoing conflict and environmental grievances may disrupt operations. Such near-term supply uncertainty is nickel-price negative.

Villagers on Indonesia’s Wawonii Island confront nickel miners as a mining boom expands Indonesia’s nickel supply chain for EV batteries. Protest actions, detentions, clashes and burned equipment raise the risk of operational disruptions and contested land, potentially affecting near-term nickel production despite growing processing capacity. | SMM reports nickel prices fluctuating with little change in spot premiums and slack intraday spot trades. NPI volumes were transacted at 1,240 yuan/mtu, but producers cut prices due to high inventories and weak markets. On demand, stainless steel sales controls are relaxed and alloy buying stays on demand, weighing on nickel. | Fathom Nickel increased its land position under the Gochager Lake option agreement by 937 hectares at Mal Lake after the optionor acquired the new disposition. The company also updated Albert Lake exploration status and expects assays later in April-May, but provides no production or reserve changes. | Flying Nickel Mining signed an Impact and Benefit Agreement with Norway House Cree Nation to advance the Minago nickel project in Manitoba. The IBA includes jobs, contracting, board participation, share subscription, and revenue-sharing based on nickel revenues, improving certainty around development. | The Mille Lacs Band of Ojibwe and allies launched the “Water Over Nickel” initiative opposing Minnesota plans for the proposed Tamarack nickel mine by Talon Metals. They cite risks to local water, watersheds, and cultural sites and urge officials to prioritize clean water over nickel mining.

SMM reports Ni 1.5% nickel ore at about $71/wmt CIF, slightly lower WoW. Philippine rainy-season conditions keep nickel ore supply tight. NPI demand from stainless producers has been weaker, pressuring NPI prices and prompting wait-and-see restocking, though limited supply suggests nickel ore may stabilize with occasional drops. | London copper fell as investors reassessed China demand prospects after early 2020s factory data grew more slowly than expected and property investment stayed weak. A steadier U.S. dollar also dampened buying interest. In parallel, LME aluminium and nickel were slightly lower on the same day. | Canada Nickel says its first metallurgical test at the Reid ultramafic nickel project near Crawford in Ontario produced a head grade of 0.35% nickel, 0.10% sulphur, and 6.2% iron, alongside reported high nickel recoveries. The results support improved prospects for future nickel output.

TMC entered a non-binding MOU with Japan’s PAMCO to evaluate toll processing 1.3 million tonnes/year of polymetallic nodules from 2025. PAMCO would use existing Hachinohe smelting infrastructure to produce nickel-copper-cobalt alloy and manganese silicate, with further study on upgrading to matte. Near-term effects are uncertain due to study-only terms. | European nickel refined premiums for uncut cathode and briquettes rose sharply, especially in-whs Rotterdam, amid tighter regional availability and higher offers/assessments. LME nickel weakness encouraged some consumer return, but liquidity remained limited at top range levels. Cathode premiums stayed high, while US premiums were mostly flat.

China imported 87,800 mt of nickel hydrometallurgy intermediate products in Dec 2022 (17,700 mt Ni content), down 13% MoM. Nickel matte imports were 30,000 mt in Dec, down 7% MoM. Tsingshan shifted some matte-based lines to NPI, cutting matte output by 9,000 mt in Jan, potentially tightening intermediate supply. | Mistango (15% interest) updates Manibridge drilling results, highlighting over 1% nickel mineralization and a highest-grade continuous intersection of 19.45m at 1.69% NiEq, with assays also incorporating Cu and Co components. These are exploration findings rather than production changes, so near-term metal price effects are likely limited. | The European Union published its Critical Raw Materials Act and designated copper and nickel as critical raw materials. The framework aims to simplify permitting and strengthen access to supply. While potentially supportive for medium-term investment certainty, the excerpt provides no immediate trade or supply shock likely to drive near-term prices. | The article argues copper should benefit from supply tightness, historically low inventories, and ongoing disruptions, reinforced by EU legislation classifying copper and nickel as critical raw materials. It also expects aluminum to remain above historical averages into early 2024. Nickel is framed as having a 2023 surplus despite EV-driven demand. | The FCA confirmed it has opened an enforcement investigation into the LME’s conduct and controls related to the LME’s 2022 suspension of nickel trading. The review follows the disorderly nickel market after Russia’s invasion and price spikes, and aims to assess governance and lessons for future market structure and risk management.

Industrial metals prices fell as investors worried that a banking crisis could worsen and hurt global economic growth. Copper eased on both LME and SHFE. Nickel also declined. Aluminium was mixed but broadly pressured, reflecting risk-off positioning as liquidity promises and a Credit Suisse rescue deal failed to calm markets. | Access World, once owned by Glencore, allegedly shipped over $1.2 million of fake nickel from Rotterdam, with LME warrants totaling 54 tonnes equivalent discovered as non-deliverable under contract specifications. The small quantity (0.14% of live stock) still raises nickel-industry fraud and liquidity concerns, likely pressuring nickel sentiment. | The article says nickel prices have fallen significantly from February to March and remain range-bound, with lower lows needed to confirm a downtrend. It notes South Africa’s load shedding threatens ferrochrome supply for stainless steel, but overall direction for nickel is unclear, with potential for stabilization. | The Mille Lacs Band of Ojibwe launched an initiative opposing a multi-billion-dollar nickel mine. By challenging the project, the action raises the risk of delays, permitting hurdles, or reduced near-term supply prospects for nickel, which may support prices. However, timing and project outcome remain uncertain.

The FCA confirmed it has opened an enforcement investigation into the LME’s decision to suspend the nickel market after disorderly conditions in March 2022, when nickel prices spiked sharply following the Russia-Ukraine invasion. The probe targets LME conduct, systems, and controls, with implications for nickel market governance. | Andrew Forrest’s Wyloo Metals has launched a $760 million takeover bid for BHP-related nickel supplier Mincor Resources, offering $1.40 per share, a 35% premium. The deal raises expectations of changes in ownership and availability of nickel feedstock, which can support nickel sentiment in the near term. | SMM says February spot ferronickel trading was dominated by supply from Indonesia and Dominica, but prices fell sharply as refined nickel and high-grade NPI slumped. Ferronickel premiums over NPI shrank, including Ni 15–20% trading near Indonesian NPI and Ni>35% premiums dropping, while Ni 25–30% premiums held up. | A Dutch warehouse mix-up led JPMorgan Chase to mistakenly buy bags believed to contain over 50 metric tons of nickel, later found to be filled with stones. The incident follows an LME nickel supply error. The amount is small versus inventories, implying limited immediate impact on nickel prices. | Reuters reports copper prices in London fell Tuesday as the dollar recovered from a five-week low and traders worried about a looming global financial crisis. While some risk appetite returned after UBS’s Credit Suisse takeover, LME three-month copper eased 0.4%. Aluminium rose slightly, while SHFE nickel fell 2.6%. | JPMorgan allegedly received bags of stones rather than the purchased nickel after the LME found inspected sacks at a Dutch warehouse failed quality standards. The misdelivered ~54 metric tons were worth about $1.3 million at current nickel prices. LME storage/checking responsibilities may face pressure. | Wyloo Metals, backed by Andrew Forrest, has bid A$760 million for Mincor Resources, prompting a 41% jump in Mincor shares. The assets feed a WA nickel smelter network tied to BHP. While it highlights growing nickel demand, it is primarily takeover-driven and not an immediate supply shock. | SMM reports that on March 21 nickel briquette prices averaged 175,350 yuan/mt, down 5,050 yuan/mt from the previous trading day. The quoted range is 175,000-175,700 yuan/mt, signaling a short-term bearish move in nickel-related pricing. | Midland Exploration begins a C$3.4m nickel-focused exploration program in Nunavik, including lake sediment and airborne ZTEM surveys targeting a crustal-scale structure favorable for Ni-Cu-Co mineralization. It builds on prior Ni-Cu discoveries and shows in grab and channel samples but provides no immediate production or supply changes. | SHFE data cited by SMM show nickel inventories dropped sharply in the week ending March 17. Stocks fell to 2,986 mt, down 31.28% from the previous week. The report implies tighter near-term availability, which can provide upward support to nickel prices. | SMM says spot nickel trading in Shanghai remained sluggish on a slight rise in Jinchuan and Nornickel premiums. Jinchuan premium averaged 7,650 yuan/mt (down 100). Nornickel averaged 2,850 yuan/mt (up 150). Nickel briquette transactions were also slack as briquette still traded at premiums over nickel sulphate. | Trafigura says bank financing for nickel trading remains robust despite fraud losses, but the LME found irregularities in nickel warrants, including non-conformant bagged briquettes discovered via inspections. The market may see more “flight to quality” and risk-off lending, heightening near-term nickel logistics and quality concerns. | Wyloo Consolidated Investments, via Wyloo Metals, launched an A$750m on-market takeover bid for Mincor Resources, offering A$1.40 cash per share for the stake it does not own. Mincor develops a nickel sulphide project in Western Australia, linking the deal to nickel supply sentiment. | Trafigura and other traders received bags of stones instead of nickel under LME contracts stored in a Rotterdam warehouse. LME found nine contracts containing material other than nickel and inspected holdings. Separately, Trafigura faces major losses from alleged nickel substitution with carbon steel, reinforcing nickel supply and quality risk.

The LME cancelled nine nickel contracts after “irregularities” at a Rotterdam warehouse, where JPMorgan was said to own the invalidated nickel warrants. The episode sparked wider inspection of LME nickel on warrant across Europe and Asia, though no further issues were found so far, keeping near-term nickel delivery uncertainty elevated. | JPMorgan owned nine LME nickel contracts that were suspended/canceled after irregularities were found at a Rotterdam warehouse, with the issue backed by stone material rather than metal. The LME and warehouses launched widespread nickel verification. While no broader problems were found, deliverability uncertainty is likely negative for nickel prices.

LME cancelled nine nickel warrants (54 tonnes) due to failures to meet contract specification after some delivered nickel was found to be stone. Concerns over additional non-conforming metal have damaged delivery confidence, widening steep discounts/contango for nearby nickel contracts versus longer-dated prices. | The LME said it found no further irregularities after cancelling nine nickel warrants (54 tonnes) for non-conforming bagged nickel containing stones. The exchange postponed nickel trading to allow warehouse inspections. Traders are rolling positions and nearby contracts show steep discounts, reflecting nervousness about LME delivery quality. | Fathom Nickel says it has acquired new mineral claims at Watts Lake in Saskatchewan through a cash-and-share deal with SKRR Exploration. The expanded land package triples coverage and connects the historic Gochager Lake nickel deposit, strengthening its nickel exploration position; the claims may also host VMS-style lead-zinc mineralization. | Rusal plans additional London lawsuits involving Nornickel CEO Vladimir Potanin, extending a long-running shareholder dispute tied to dividend protections and alleged diversion of Nornickel subsidiaries. Rusal says action protects shareholders, while sanctions apply to Potanin but not directly to Nornickel. No operational impact is indicated. | LME said that, after completing inspections of bagged nickel warrants in licensed warehouses for irregularities in stocks, Asian-hours nickel trading will resume on Monday (March 27). Trading had been postponed by about a week after the LME found nickel failing to meet contract specifications at a warehouse. | LME said Asian-hours nickel trading will resume on March 27 after a full inspection of bagged nickel warrants in LME-licensed warehouses. Trading had been postponed by a week after nickel failing to meet contract specifications was found earlier. The decision follows irregularities in warehouse stocks and LME contract compliance checks. | The LME said a 100% inspection of all bagged nickel warrants in licensed warehouses found no further irregularities. It expects to resume Asian-hours nickel trading on March 27. The update follows last week’s cancellation of some nickel warrants amid concerns about contaminated or incorrect contents. | Wood Mackenzie says China’s renewed growth would most benefit steel, aluminium and copper, with housing/property recovery lifting construction-linked demand. In a high-growth scenario it estimates additional copper and aluminium consumption and suggests that higher energy demand could trigger supply curtailments that can support nickel and other metals. | Wood Mackenzie expects China’s reopening and faster growth to raise construction-driven demand for copper and aluminium, with additional tonnes forecast for both. In a higher-growth scenario, exporters and stronger activity could support metal prices. It also warns higher energy demand may trigger supply curtailments, potentially boosting nickel as well.

SMM reports that as of 10:06:35 on Mar 24, the most-traded SHFE nickel contract was quoted at 177,670 yuan/mt, up 1.0% over 19 minutes. The short-term increase suggests bullish intraday price momentum for nickel on the Shanghai futures exchange. | ASX-listed Dynamic Metals says it has made a new nickel sulphide discovery, with drill results suggesting a largely continuous 30m mineralised hit. A 3m section returned no ore due to technical issues, but the finding could still support expectations for future nickel supply. | Copper prices eased from a three-week high as markets weighed banking-sector turbulence and the Fed’s cautious stance after a rate hike. A steadier dollar limited further gains. The article also notes mild moves in aluminium and stronger nickel on SHFE, reflecting broader risk sentiment rather than a new supply-demand shock. | Copper prices rose to a three-week high for a second day as banking-sector unease kept the U.S. dollar near seven-week lows. Reuters cites supportive FX effects for dollar-priced commodities and expectations around the Fed’s rate path. Aluminium and nickel were also higher on the day. | GEM plans a South Korea nickel battery-material joint venture, investing about 1.21 trillion won to build capacity of at least 43,000 tonnes per year by 2026. The project targets U.S. EV tax credit rules under the IRA, shifting sourcing to North America allies and boosting nickel-linked demand. | GEM, SK On and ECOPRO Materials plan a South Korea joint venture investing about 1.21 trillion won to build capacity for 43,000 tonnes of nickel-based battery materials annually. The project is designed to satisfy U.S. Inflation Reduction Act sourcing rules for EV tax credits, boosting expected nickel-related demand. | SMM reports bonded zone nickel inventory fell 300 mt week-on-week to 4,600 mt as of March 24, with briquettes at 1,370 mt and plates at 3,230 mt. Despite inventories declining in line with expectations, nickel prices remain under pressure at lows. Downstream buying is on demand, while new-energy briquette demand stays weak. | SMM reports Chinese nickel ore inventories fell 185,000 wmt week-on-week to 6.41 million wmt as of March 24, with Ni content of 50,000 mt. Port stocks also declined at seven major ports. Nickel ore prices dropped on lower NPI prices, but buyers expect further reductions as Philippines rains continue. | GlobalData says Russia is the third-largest nickel producer, with 2023 output slightly down versus 2022, and exports up about 2% in 2023. It expects Russian nickel exports to decline modestly (CAGR ~1.14%) from 2023-2027. No immediate supply shock is described.

SMM reported that pure nickel inventories in six China regions fell by 379 mt to 5,670 mt as of March 24. Nickel briquette inventories were down 50 mt to 580 mt, while nickel plate inventories declined 379 mt to 5,090 mt, driven by downstream demand for on-demand purchases. | Nickel prices rose 4.82% last week despite a Fed rate hike that was not initially bullish. The report links the rally to tightening Chinese spot supply as pure nickel imports incurred losses, while demand for pure nickel stayed weak. Stainless steel mill cut news weighed on NPI, but overall nickel support prevailed. | Reuters reports copper holding a narrow range with small gains on both LME and SHFE as investors weigh global economic headwinds and banking-system worries. LME nickel resumes Asian hours after a prior suspension and cancellations, while LME aluminium rises modestly. Overall moves are mild, implying limited net impact. | Copper traded in a tight range as investors weighed global economic headwinds and banking risks, with LME copper up 0.3%. LME nickel resumed Asian hours after a year-long suspension, but fell 0.6%. SHFE nickel rose sharply, while aluminium was up modestly. | Copper prices held in a tight range, with three-month LME copper up 0.3%. Nickel resumed LME Asian trading after a long suspension; LME nickel fell 0.6% while SHFE nickel rose 3.3%. Aluminium also inched higher on both LME and SHFE amid macro and bank-stability worries. | Copper prices were steady-to-slightly higher on the LME and SHFE as investors weighed metal stocks against global economic headwinds and concerns over the banking system. LME nickel resumed Asian hours after a prior suspension, but fell on the day. Aluminium rose modestly on both exchanges. | Copper held a tight range but posted small gains as falling inventories and improving demand outweighed macro worries. LME nickel slipped after resuming Asian-hours trading, with concerns over liquidity, margins and deliverable constraints. Aluminium was also slightly higher on both LME and SHFE, supported by broader metal firmness. | Reuters reports LME/SHFE copper trading slightly higher on lower inventories and improving demand sentiment despite macro concerns. LME nickel resumes Asian-hours trading after a suspension, but the contract slipped, reflecting volatile/liquidity risks. SHFE aluminium also gained modestly alongside broader base-metals moves. | Copper traded in a tight range but was slightly higher, supported by falling inventories, improving demand, and expectations around a possible pause in U.S. rate hikes. LME nickel resumed Asian hours after a suspension, with the contract slightly lower and liquidity concerns highlighted. Aluminium rose marginally alongside broader metal sentiment. | Copper prices were steady to slightly higher on Monday, supported by falling inventories, improving demand, and a potential pause in U.S. interest-rate hikes. LME nickel resumed Asian-hours trading after a long suspension, easing logistics for arbitrage, though liquidity remains limited. Aluminium also edged up, but without clear catalysts. | LS Group completed construction of a nickel sulfate plant in Asan, operated via affiliate Toricom, producing 5,000 tons for EV batteries. The company plans to expand nickel sulfate capacity to 270,000 tons by 2030 using additional feedstocks, supporting LS MnM’s battery materials value chain and potential nickel demand. | Palladium One reported discovery of the Ember Zone, a high-grade nickel–copper target at its Tyko project in Ontario, with earlier soil/VTEM indications and plans for further geophysical work and drilling. The news may support longer-term Ni/Cu sentiment but lacks immediate supply or production impacts. | Palladium One Mining reports discovery of the “Ember Zone,” a new high-grade nickel–copper mineralized zone at its Tyko project in Ontario, adjacent to interpreted feeder dyke/chonolith structures. The company describes initial geophysical/soil anomalies, planned BHEM surveys, and ongoing exploration drilling. | Minnesota’s DNR will hold a hearing on Monday over a key permit for PolyMet’s NorthMet proposed copper-nickel mine. The review focuses on whether a bentonite-based waste-rock dam design will work, amid ongoing environmental and water-quality permitting disputes that could delay supply. | Minnesota’s DNR will hold a hearing on Monday for a key permit for PolyMet’s NorthMet project, intended to be the state’s first copper-nickel mine. The decision hinges on whether bentonite will work for waste-rock containment. Permitting progress could support near-term copper and nickel sentiment. | The LME said that nickel bags stored in a licensed warehouse contained stones rather than actual nickel. The incident follows previous nickel-related troubles, raising concerns about stock integrity and warehouse controls. Such issues can weigh on confidence and increase perceived risk in near-term nickel supply pricing. | Palladium One announced the discovery of new high-grade nickel in a zone that includes copper. While exploration results may not immediately change production, the news can modestly support sentiment for both nickel and copper prices over the coming days as traders reassess potential future supply. | The LME discovered irregularities in nine nickel contracts after inspections found some delivered “nickel” bags filled with stones. Rotterdam warehouse receipts and warrants tied to JPMorgan were suspended/invalidated, raising delivery/security concerns. Nickel for immediate delivery reportedly plunged to the widest discount to futures in 15 years, pressuring prices. | The LME found “irregularities” in nine nickel contracts invalidated after bags labeled as nickel in Rotterdam were found to contain stones. Warehouse staff are inspecting thousands of bags, and traders are reassessing delivery security. Immediate nickel prices reportedly plunged to the widest discount to 3-month futures in 15 years.

Indonesia nickel company Merdeka Battery Materials plans an $580 million IPO in April, selling about 10.24% of shares at 780–795 rupiah, with an additional 1.01% if oversubscribed. The Reuters report is based on the prospectus and does not detail production or supply changes affecting nickel near term. | Indonesia’s Merdeka Battery Materials plans a $580 million IPO to raise funds for loan repayment, capex and working capital, and to develop the first phase of a high pressure acid leach (HPAL) nickel plant (60,000 t/y). The financing supports processing expansion in a major nickel hub, likely lifting nickel sentiment. | SMM reports Mar 28 Jinchuan nickel premiums at 6,300–6,600 yuan/mt, down 150 yuan/mt, while NORNICKEL premiums rose to an average 2,750 yuan/mt. Nickel briquette prices eased and trading remained slack because briquettes still traded at premiums to nickel sulphate. Nickel is rebounding from lows despite weaker premiums. | POSCO Holdings signed an MOA with Ningbo Richin to build nickel intermediate (MHP) production in Indonesia, targeting 60,000 tons capacity to start this year and production in 2025. The project supports nickel sulfate and battery cathode supply, leveraging Indonesia’s large nickel resources. | Copper held steady to slightly higher in Asia after a deal to buy SVB’s assets improved risk appetite, supporting dollar-priced metals. However, gains were capped by weak Chinese demand tied to overseas macro factors and higher prices. Aluminium and nickel also rose modestly, reflecting the same sentiment move. | Palladium One reports discovery of a new high-grade “Ember Zone” at its Tyko nickel-copper project in Ontario, with multiple near-surface drill intercepts containing both Ni and Cu. The find is adjacent to interpreted chonolith/feeder dyke structures and the Cupa Lake VTEM/soil anomaly, but it remains early-stage. | Palladium One Mining announced discovery of the “Ember Zone,” a new high-grade nickel-copper target on its Tyko Project in Ontario, with drill intercepts reporting up to ~2% Ni and ~2.8% Cu. The company links the find to a broader feeder dyke/Chonolith system near Cupa Lake anomalies. | Nickel prices rose to a three-week high on the LME, supported by short-covering and dwindling inventories, including a 28% weekly fall in SFE-linked warehouse stocks. Gains were capped by expectations of increased supply from Indonesia. The article frames a rebound driven by tightness and positioning.  | India’s Jindal Stainless signed a $157 million deal to build and run a nickel pig iron facility in Indonesia, targeting up to 200,000 t/year to cover its nickel pig iron needs. The move follows India’s nickel ore shortfall and comes as nickel prices recently rebounded after a sharp slump. | Jindal Stainless will buy a 49% stake in Indonesia’s New Yaking and build a nickel pig iron plant in Halmahera with up to 200,000 tonnes annual capacity to secure its nickel needs. The piece discusses copper and nickel outlooks via broker commentary. BHP lowered its production targets as its Australian iron ore and coking coal division moves toward the bottom of its ranges. The SHFE/LME nickel price ratio declined and the import window stayed closed, indicating tighter near-term flows, though longer-term contracts may raise future arrivals. | Norilsk Nickel said Q1 2023 palladium and platinum output rose, while nickel production fell 9% year on year due to scheduled furnace and mill turnarounds. The company also cited improved nickel cathode quality from operational efficiency. | The LME appointed an interim chairman amid fallout from its 2022 nickel trading suspension, FCA scrutiny, and lawsuits over canceled trades. The exchange says it will oversee the market as nickel volumes slump and daily price limits remain for nickel, copper, and aluminium trading. | The Biden administration’s proposed vehicle pollution standards aim to electrify a majority of new U.S. vehicle sales within the next decade. If finalized, the policy would increase expected demand for EV “green” metals, including copper and nickel, through higher battery and electrical component requirements.

SMM data shows China’s pure nickel inventory rose to 4,936 mt (+161 WoW) alongside higher nickel briquette stocks (+380) and lower nickel plate stocks (-220). Spot nickel prices continued rising, while downstream demand stayed cautious. The closed import window limited new supply, and briquettes may flow into the market from active salt factories. | SMM reports battery-grade nickel sulphate prices in Shanghai fell WoW but the decline slowed due to a wider gap between buyer offers and seller quotes and weak demand from ternary precursor producers. A larger nickel sulphate–pure nickel spread drew traders, and a near-term rebound is expected. | SMM reports Ni 1.5% ore averaging $50/wmt CIF, down $2/wmt week on week, as Philippine mine shipments stay low and tighten China’s supply. A Chinese-flagged bulk carrier carrying 55,000 metric tons of nickel ore ran aground off Guiuan in Eastern Samar after strong waves. Stellantis reportedly signed an offtake agreement and invested in an alliance supplying battery-grade nickel and cobalt sulphate. Stellantis also invests in Alliance Nickel, supporting demand expectations for nickel sulphate supply. | Copper prices steadied on the LME after China pledged to boost growth, but weak demand and recession/banking-stress fears capped gains. IGO agreed to farm into Venus Metals’ Henderson nickel-lithium project in Western Australia, funding exploration through a pre-feasibility study and potentially acquiring up to 70% of the tenements. The nickel forward curve remains in persistent contango and contracts show liquidity concerns and potential volatility. However, weak demand keeps physical premiums under pressure globally. | Posco will invest $440m to build a nickel smelter in Indonesia’s Halmahera, targeting 52,000 tons per year of nickel matte (70–75% nickel) for EV battery intermediates. The project follows Indonesia’s export restrictions aimed at boosting downstream processing and expands Posco’s nickel supply ambitions. | Satellite-observed smelter activity fell sharply in April, with global copper dispersion at its lowest since March 2021 and additional planned shutdowns/maintenance in Indonesia and Bulgaria. Marex/SAVANT link weaker smelting activity to higher treatment and refining charges and an inverse relationship with LME copper, pointing to near-term bearish pressure. Nickel dispersion also slipped. | SMM notes crude oil prices gained over 1% and the U.S. dollar index edged lower. In the reported session, LME metals rose across the board: copper +0.68%, nickel +0.65%, and aluminium +0.52%. SHFE showed mixed moves, with nickel sharply lower and copper/aluminium slightly down.

SHFE nickel traded rangebound as downstream firms were cautious about restocking after Labour Day. NPI holders stayed slower to ship despite profits. Mild gains in stainless steel spot and HRC aided demand, but #316L fell sharply. Overall tight domestic and abroad nickel spot supply keeps prices supported, so SHFE nickel should remain rangebound. | Brokers flag pressure across industrial raw materials. Citi turns bearish on copper, expecting slower China demand and weaker US/Europe consumption amid high inventories and disappointing orders, trimming the 0–3 month copper forecast. Macquarie expects a large global nickel surplus, with nickel pig iron and sulphate oversupply widening discounts versus LME. | Citi turns bearish on copper, citing softer global PMI readings, weaker China consumption expectations, high/rising inventories in the US and Europe, and continued order disappointment, cutting its near-term copper estimate. Separately, Macquarie expects a large nickel production surplus, with nickel pig iron and sulphate in oversupply and trading at discounts. | Copper steadied but sentiment weakened as China data pointed to softer growth and demand, with April imports down 12.5% and manufacturing activity slipping. LME-approved copper inventories rose 40% since April 18 and a firmer dollar weighed on prices. Nickel fell on rising supplies; aluminium edged up modestly. | Environmental group WALHI and others urge Tesla to stop or limit direct investment and use of Indonesia’s nickel, citing pollution, smelter emissions, and alleged rights violations affecting fishing communities. With Indonesia a major nickel supplier, the scrutiny could increase compliance or disruption risk, weighing on nickel market sentiment.

Copper prices rose on a weaker U.S. dollar ahead of U.S. inflation data, but gains were limited by expectations of subdued demand in Q2 and rising supply. Peru’s March copper output jumped 20.4% after mine stoppages. Aluminium and nickel were roughly steady to mixed as traders waited for CPI. | SMM reports China’s refined nickel output at 17,600 mt in April, slightly higher month-on-month, after offsetting shutdown/overhaul effects with production increases in Jiangsu. May output is forecast to rise to 17,900 mt, supported by continued Jiangsu ramp-ups and normal operations in Guangxi, suggesting added supply pressure. | SMM reported that May 10 spot nickel transactions were sluggish. Jinchuan nickel premiums rose slightly, but NORNICKEL premiums fell, with nickel prices moving down. Nickel briquette trades were slack and priced at premiums over nickel sulphate, reflecting weaker near-term demand conditions for nickel. | Macarthur Minerals provides an update on the Lake Giles tenements, where ultramafic komatiite-type rocks and serpentinised olivine/talc-carbonate formations are viewed as having high potential for nickel sulphides. The company plans nickel-targeted drilling, EM surveys, and assaying to refine prospects, indicating improved exploration focus. | Québec Nickel Corp. initiated scoping metallurgical tests at its Ducros project to assess whether the Fortin Sill Zone can produce a saleable Ni-Cu-Co-PGE+Au concentrate. SGS will run grindability, flotation, and separation tests using 150 kg of drill core, with results expected later in Q4 2023.

China’s GEM plans a $500 million joint project in Indonesia’s Morowali Industrial Park to produce 20,000 tonnes per year of nickel intermediate product for the new energy sector. GEM holds 51%, CAHAYA JAYA INVESTMENT 26%, and Weiming 18%. The announcement links directly to nickel supply expectations. | SMM reports May 11 nickel market weakness. Jinchuan nickel premiums averaged 8,900 yuan/mt, while NORNICKEL premiums fell to about 6,100 yuan/mt. Nickel price pressure is linked to poor downstream demand and recession-hit trading, with higher nickel warrants and slack briquette transactions pushing premiums down. | Pan Global said it has secured surface access to begin drilling immediately at the Romana West target within its Escacena project in Spain, aiming to test an on-strike extension of the La Romana copper-tin-silver discovery. Two rigs will mobilize and 25–30 holes are planned, with results pending.

Mallee Resources says its Avebury nickel mine has exited care-and-maintenance and is ramping up. By end of March it had about 210 staff and produced and sold the first 10,000 tonnes of nickel-cobalt concentrate. Output is building toward nameplate capacity (900,000 tpa), supporting higher near-term supply. | SMM reports SHFE nickel weakened on poor downstream demand and recession-driven trades, with rising nickel warrants and falling spot premiums. NPI supply is increasing as more Indonesian shipments arrive, while low-inventory holders delay sales. Stainless steel futures slid with nickel, pulling spot slightly lower. SMM expects nickel to trade rangebound today. | Copper was up on Friday but set for its biggest weekly drop since November as weak global economic data darkened demand expectations and rising supplies pressured prices. LME copper inventories rose toward a two-month high and a stronger dollar hurt sentiment, weighing on the broader metals complex including nickel and aluminium. | Nickel ore inventories at Chinese ports fell 243,000 wmt WoW to 6.36 million wmt as of May 12, with total Ni content at 50,000 mt. Ore prices rose slightly. Buyers returned to rigid-demand purchases, while mines planned higher quotes; Philippine spot shipments resume despite poor weather, keeping supply expectations tighter. | SMM said May 12 Jinchuan nickel premiums rose on supply tightness, but spot trading was average because futures prices are low. NORNICKEL premiums also edged up. Nickel briquette prices declined and deal activity was slack, with briquettes still trading at premiums over nickel sulphate. | Copper hit the lowest level in over five months as fears of slowing demand in China, rising LME warehouse inventories, and a stronger U.S. dollar pressured prices. The sell-off was reinforced by recent Chinese inflation data. Aluminium was slightly higher on the day, while nickel’s gains reflected short-covering amid expected surpluses. | Norilsk Nickel said Rotterdam has allowed it to continue selling nickel through its transshipment hub after earlier disruptions. The company is also using the Tangier port in Morocco for product logistics. Continued nickel deliveries point to easing supply constraints, likely supporting nickel prices in the coming days. | The article says the LME will require warehouses to verify (“feel”) their nickel bags following a heist. The change targets accountability and reduces uncertainty about physical nickel availability against LME warrants. Such operational disruptions can tighten deliverable supplies and support short-term nickel prices. | The LME plans tighter rules for how licensed warehouses handle nickel, including magnet/metal-detector tests, “touch inspections,” and weighing bags before delivery out of the LME system. The move follows cases where nickel delivered to traders proved to be stone and earlier irregularities in LME-warranted stocks.

Benchmark London copper prices fell in Asian trading as the U.S. dollar firmed to a five-week high and investors worried about weaker China demand ahead of industrial activity data. Rising inventories and a slowdown in global growth pressured sentiment. LME aluminium and nickel also declined. | Copper rebounded after a five-month low as China’s PBOC injected liquidity, signaling support for economic growth and improving base-metal sentiment. The weaker dollar and “bargain hunting” aided prices. Aluminium rose on lower LME on-warrant stocks, while nickel weakened on vulnerability concerns and forecast cuts. | Emerita Resources reported assay results from 21 drill holes at its Iberian Belt West La Infanta deposit, highlighting polymetallic massive sulphide intervals including ~1.4% copper over 4.2m in one hole. The company also received a permit for Infanta Sur to start exploration. No nickel or aluminium impact is indicated. | Nickel Asia reported Q1 net income down 8%, citing foreign exchange losses and a lower weighted average nickel ore price. The move suggests weaker nickel ore pricing during the quarter, which can influence short-term sentiment about nickel supply/demand and producer cost conditions.

Qianhai Mercantile Exchange plans to launch contracts for lithium carbonate, nickel sulphate, and possibly one other battery metal, alongside considering a low-carbon “green aluminium” physical contract in China. The move targets EV-battery demand and rising preferences for lower-emissions aluminium, but no near-term supply or inventory change is cited. | SMM reports that Chinese ports received 2.67 million wmt of nickel ore in April, up 42% month-on-month, while Philippine exports rose to 2.36 million wmt, up 52%. The monsoon disruption eased and mining recovered, with demand picking up at the same time. May shipments are expected to rise further. | China’s nickel ore imports rebounded in March to 2.12 million mt (+42.9% MoM), with laterite ore and higher shipments from the Philippines after monsoon disruptions. Nickel sulphide ore imports also rose. Domestic port inventories increased, and April imports are expected to grow as NPI supply tightens. | A Thomson Reuters Foundation report warns that nickel mining for EV battery supply chains in Indonesia and the Philippines involves environmental damage and alleged human-rights abuses. It notes Indonesia’s nickel ore export restrictions, Philippines mine closure/resumption history, and recent pledges to improve monitoring—raising reputational and compliance risks for nickel producers. | China’s nickel imports fell sharply in March. Refined nickel totaled 4,443 mt, down 52% MoM and 77% YoY, while nickel powder and flake reached 1,310 mt, down 35% MoM and 58% YoY. Nickel briquettes rose MoM but fell YoY; Russia nickel plate imports dropped to zero. | China imported 129,000 mt of hydrometallurgical intermediate nickel products in March, up 13% month-on-month (equivalent to 24,600 mt Ni). High-nickel matte imports reached 20,000 mt and were up year-on-year, with Indonesia driving most growth. This suggests firmer nickel material inflows for processing. | Palladium One Mining announced initial results from 2023 exploration at its Tyko nickel-copper project in Ontario, using updated airborne magnetic survey data to refine chonolith/feeder dyke targeting and begin field mapping and sampling. The release is exploratory with no immediate production or supply disruption mentioned. | Churchill Resources announced a NI 43-101 technical report for its Florence Lake nickel project, identifying 43 high-priority nickel targets and recommending a 2023 field program and later drilling. It also updates plans for the Taylor Brook project with deeper drilling under prior high-grade Ni/Cu intersections. No production or confirmed new resources.

SMM’s daily wrap shows most SHFE/LME base metals closed lower. SHFE nickel fell over 3% and LME nickel fell 0.9%, while SHFE copper and LME copper dropped about 0.7–0.9%. Aluminium was the exception: SHFE aluminium rose ~0.4% and LME aluminium edged up ~0.24%. | Wave Nickel agreed to buy Brazil’s Niquelândia nickel mine and processing plant from CBA to produce nickel MHP using a new microwave process. Output ramp-up is gradual, starting with a demonstration plant by end-2024 and full scale later, with potential cost and emissions advantages. Nickel sulfate/MHP prices have been under pressure. | Canada announced nearly CAD 360 million to support research, development and expansion in key minerals, including copper, nickel and aluminium, and plans to simplify mining licensing. It also issued a Canada-U.S. joint statement on building a key mineral supply chain, potentially improving medium-term supply expectations and sentiment. | St-Georges Eco-Mining reported partial re-assay results for drill hole MN22-03 at its Manicouagan project, including high-grade nickel and copper intersections alongside platinum-group metals. The update improves geological confidence for future resource potential, but remains exploration-focused with no immediate supply impact on near-term copper or nickel prices. | Nickel 28 reported fiscal 2023 results for its 8.56% stake in the Ramu nickel-cobalt operation in Papua New Guinea. The company cited strong production and sales, lower average cash costs, significant debt repayment, and expectations of continued strength in 2023 as EV demand for nickel-rich batteries improves. | St-Georges Eco-Mining reported re-assay results for drill hole MN22-03 at its Manicouagan project, including intervals averaging 2.43% nickel and 1.18% copper, with individual quarter-meter samples up to about 3.36% copper and 4.1% nickel. The update boosts exploration sentiment for both metals. | XCana Petroleum Corp. reports completing an 80-acre expansion to gain full ownership of the historically copper-producing Boyer Copper Deposit, adjacent to its Nevada Nickel #73 claim. The company frames the “Table Mountain Critical Mineral Project” as targeting copper and nickel among others, but offers no production timelines.

Nickel Industries is profiled as demand for nickel rises, driven by stainless steel and EV-related expectations. However, the piece warns about regulatory risk in Indonesia (processing rules, potential emissions sanctions) and ESG/low-carbon nickel requirements that could pressure its coal-based production. Net effect on near-term nickel pricing is mixed. | SMM reports spot nickel premiums rose amid low supply while nickel futures fell sharply. With economic recession limiting demand, trades remain weak even as spot activity picks up. However, expected growth in supply—driven by spot inflows for warrant delivery—should push stainless steel spot prices lower, weighing on nickel. | Copper prices fell in London as a stronger U.S. dollar reached seven-week highs and LME-registered on-warrant stocks rose to a six-month peak. The article also describes weakness across base metals, with nickel touching a fresh multi-month low and aluminium trading lower, reflecting tighter financial conditions and softer demand expectations. | Nickel 28 filed its management information circular and proxy materials for its June 12 shareholder meeting, alongside a board letter urging votes for its nominees amid a takeover attempt by Pelham. The release reiterates the company’s nickel-cobalt exposure via its Ramu joint venture and royalties, but provides no operational or supply changes. | WBMS reported March 2023 copper cathode output below consumption, leaving a supply shortage of 74,400 mt, while primary aluminium posted oversupply of 148,200 mt and refined nickel oversupply of 5,600 mt. These figures point to tight copper but substantial surplus conditions in aluminium and nickel. | WBMS reported March 2023 copper cathode output of 2.1Mt versus consumption of 2.17Mt, leaving a 74,400t supply shortage. Primary aluminium ran in oversupply (148,200t), and nickel also showed a small oversupply. Overall, the copper deficit is the clearest near-term supportive factor. | Sherritt reported a worker death at its Moa nickel/cobalt joint venture in Cuba, the second fatality in months. The company says injuries resulted from a fall and that it is implementing immediate safety measures while working with authorities. Moa mines nickel laterite for Sherritt refining, but no production downtime is stated. | The UK introduced sanctions prohibiting imports of Russian-origin copper, aluminum, and nickel, alongside a wider ban on Russian diamonds. The measure directly restricts Russian metal supply into the UK/market, raising expectations of tighter availability and supporting near-term price strength for industrial metals. | Rishi Sunak at the G7 summit in Japan announces expanded sanctions banning imports of Russian-origin copper, aluminium and nickel, alongside a wider package restricting Russian diamonds. Such measures reduce access to Russian metal supplies for the market and can tighten short-term availability, potentially lifting prices. | Rishi Sunak says G7 leaders will announce wider sanctions on Russia, including a ban on Russian-origin imports of copper, aluminium and nickel. The measures aim to restrict financing and war capacity tied to Russian supply chains, with the UK and allies expected to announce further steps over the weekend. | The UK government announced sanctions banning imports of Russian-origin copper, aluminium, and nickel, alongside Russian diamonds, as part of wider restrictions on 86 Russian individuals and firms linked to Russia’s military-industrial complex. The UK’s import ban is expected to tighten supply availability and support prices for the affected metals. | Rishi Sunak said the G7 will ban or restrict imports of Russian diamonds and also extend sanctions to Russian-origin copper, aluminium and nickel. The measures aim to pressure Russia after the invasion of Ukraine. The news signals tighter trade access for Russian metal supply into allied markets.

Reuters reports analysts expect global nickel prices to fall as Indonesia expands nickel capacity and production. Indonesia’s output growth in NPI and MHP is forecast to relieve supply constraints, while production costs decline. CRU and others project the LME three-month nickel contract dropping this year and below $20,000 in 2024. | UK Prime Minister Rishi Sunak announced an embargo on imports of Russian diamonds and also copper, aluminium and nickel. Reporting indicates additional restrictions on Russian diamond trade and sanctions targeting firms tied to Russia’s military-industrial sector. The move is aimed at cutting Russian economic inputs into global markets. | The UK Prime Minister announced an embargo banning imports of Russian diamonds and also Russian copper, aluminium, and nickel. UK reporting says sanctions include a broader market freeze and additional measures. Such trade restrictions on Russian metal supply can tighten availability and provide upward price pressure for copper, aluminium, and nickel. | The UK Prime Minister is reported to have announced an embargo on imports of Russian diamonds, including copper, aluminium, and nickel. The measure is part of sanctions related to Russia’s actions in Ukraine, alongside asset freezes and individual/entity restrictions. The EU is also considering diamond trade restrictions. | Copper is set for a fifth straight weekly decline as the U.S. dollar stays near two-month highs and markets price in additional Fed rate hikes. Weak Chinese demand dampens sentiment. Aluminium and nickel show intraday gains, but the broader macro backdrop caps industrial metals, leaving weekly momentum bearish. | SMM reported that spot premiums for pure nickel rose on May 19, with Jinchuan premiums averaging 11,650 yuan/mt and NORNICKEL averaging 6,900 yuan/mt. The publication links the premium gains to improving transactions and tighter pure nickel supply, indicating stronger near-term nickel market sentiment. | Ahead of the G7 Japan summit, the UK is set to unveil sanctions and export controls targeting Russia. Trade Data Monitor shows Britain imported little or no Russian copper, but did import unwrought aluminium and refined nickel from Russia in 2022-2023. Such curbs can reduce Russian metal flows and affect short-term pricing expectations. | Britain and other G7 states plan new sanctions and export controls against Russia. Factbox data shows UK Russian imports of refined copper were effectively absent in 2021 and tiny via 15,252 tonnes last year; aluminium imports from Russia fell versus 2021; refined nickel imports were much lower or absent in earlier periods. | The article outlines Britain’s recent imports of sanctioned Russian metals before new G7 sanctions and export controls are announced in Japan. It notes some Russian-origin refined copper was imported previously (none in 2021/2022 refined), aluminium supply from Russia, and Russian refined nickel. Curbs on such flows can tighten supply and lift prices. | UK Prime Minister Sunak plans new import bans on Russian copper, aluminium and nickel as part of fresh G7 sanctions. The factbox details limited UK prior imports from Russia (nickel none in early 2023; aluminium and copper past volumes). Reduced Russian trade is likely to tighten supply and support prices. | The UK said it will announce new import bans on Russian copper, aluminium and nickel alongside fresh G7 sanctions at a summit in Japan. Trade data show the UK imported limited Russian volumes—none for refined copper, none early-2023 for aluminium and nickel—while refined nickel and unwrought aluminium imports occurred in 2022. | The UK plans new import bans on Russian copper, aluminium and nickel, alongside new G7 sanctions targeting Russia over the war in Ukraine. Britain imported no Russian refined copper in recent years, while it bought unwrought aluminium and some refined nickel from Russia in 2022. The bans could tighten supply expectations. | Copper rebounded 1.2% on LME on optimism that U.S. debt talks will avert a default, lifting broader risk assets and supported by a slightly weaker dollar. The article also notes gains in aluminium and a strong rise in nickel, reflecting macro-driven sentiment despite headwinds from China demand and recession risks. | Copper rebounded as investors grew more optimistic that U.S. debt talks will avert a federal default. LME three-month copper rose about 1% and Comex futures gained, supported by a softer dollar and traders covering shorts. The piece also flags headwinds from weak China demand and recession risks ahead. | The article notes that LME profits have fallen to an 8-year low as a “nickel crisis” hits the exchange. While details are limited, the framing suggests significant stress in LME nickel markets, which usually implies near-term weakness for nickel prices due to trading disruptions and higher risk. | FPX Nickel says hydrometallurgical testwork at its Baptiste project produced battery-grade nickel sulphate and improved the refinery flowsheet with lower purification stages and reagent use. It also yielded cobalt and copper by-products. Results support inclusion in the feasibility study and potential future nickel supply for EV batteries.

Reuters reports copper fell on deteriorating demand expectations in China, alongside rising supplies and climbing LME stocks (up 80% since mid-April). Mine resumptions in Peru and a DRC royalty agreement supporting copper exports eased supply worries, widening the cash discount. Other metals were mixed; aluminum slid and nickel edged up. | Australian Adavale Resources secured a prospecting licence for its Kabanga Jirani nickel project in Tanzania and plans additional drilling, DHEM, and AMT surveys. The project is within the Luhuma trend with historic nickel sulphide intersections, while recent work also defined copper-bearing soil anomalies near an adjacent Kabanga deposit.

Atlantic Nickel said Santa Rita delivered strong Q1 2023 performance: higher nickel-in-concentrate production, lower C1 cash costs, and shipments with an average realized nickel price around $10.63/lb. It also holds forward coverage at >$13/lb on much of 2023 nickel output, with some copper forward exposure. | Copper touched a near six-month low as speculators boosted bearish positions over recession risks and weaker China demand. The cash–three-month spread widened sharply, signaling ample near-term supply, while a firm dollar and fragile risk sentiment weighed on industrial metals broadly, including aluminium and nickel, which also declined. | Australia’s Widgie Nickel raised about A$12m ($7.9m) via a discounted share placement to fund exploration, evaluation, and ongoing development at the Mt Edwards nickel project. The company cites nickel commercialisation and ongoing work at Mt Edwards, which hosts substantial nickel sulphide resources. | The G7/EU/US announced fresh sanctions aimed at closing Russia-related loopholes, with metals explicitly targeted. Russia’s large roles in global aluminium output and LME aluminium stocks raise warrant and delivery risks, while traders may pull non-Russian inventory. Russia’s copper and nickel supply shares add further near-term tightening expectations for LME base metals. | Goldman Sachs lowered its 2023 price forecasts for aluminium to $2,431/mt (from $2,700) and copper to $8,689/mt (from $9,750). It also cautioned that nickel could fall sharply amid surging supply from Indonesia and China, pointing to softer expected prices and downside risk for the metal complex. | Goldman Sachs cut its 2023 price forecasts, lowering aluminium to $2,431/mt from $2,700/mt and copper to $8,689/mt from $9,750/mt, while still expecting 12-month targets to materialize. It also flagged potential sharp nickel downside as supply from Indonesia and China rises.  | Goldman Sachs lowered its 2023 forecasts for aluminium and copper and reiterated 12-month targets may still materialise later. It highlighted potential sharp declines in nickel due to increased supply from Indonesia and China. Overall, the revised outlook weakens near-term price expectations across aluminium, copper and nickel.

Congo plans to boost its stake and control in the Sicomines infrastructure-for-minerals deal with China, seeking higher equity for Gecamines, a larger infrastructure loan envelope, and lump-sum compensation over alleged undervaluation of copper and cobalt sales. Negotiations with Chinese firms are expected to intensify after Tshisekedi returns from China. | Lifezone’s Kabanga nickel project in Tanzania (with BHP holding a minority stake) is in discussions to sell yet-to-be-produced nickel via offtake/prepayment contracts with EV and battery firms. Talks show strong interest and may support expanding refined output plans, but no agreements are finalized. | SPC Nickel said drilling at its West Graham Project in Ontario found a new high-grade intersection in hole WG-23-042, grading 1.71% nickel and 0.46% copper over 20 metres. Phase 1 drilling has completed over 5,000 metres across 27 holes, with 11 awaiting assay results. | Nickel 28 responds to a takeover-related proxy challenge, arguing Pelham lacks a credible plan. The company emphasizes its nickel exposure via its Ramu nickel-cobalt joint venture and mentions cash-flow leverage to nickel and copper prices. The article is mainly corporate governance, without new operational or supply developments.

NORNICKEL nickel premiums rose as market supply tightened, with SMM expecting tightness to last until next week. Yet trader quotations were lowered amid growing stocks, while stainless steel spot pricing weakened due to poor demand and high inventories. With imported inflows expected, nickel is forecast to move rangebound. | Miners argue nickel pricing mechanisms have not kept up with the shift from traditional stainless-steel buyers to EV and battery cathode demand. As a result, they see a window where nickel assets could be acquired cheaply, reflecting subdued nickel pricing conditions and potential near-term downward pressure on nickel. | EIA reported a much larger-than-expected fall in US crude inventories, while the dollar rose for several days. Metals trading closed mixed: SHFE nickel surged 3.08% and copper dipped 0.77%, with aluminium down 0.4%. LME nickel gained 2.33%, copper rose 0.34%, and aluminium ended flat. | SMM says May 25 spot pure nickel premiums for Jinchuan are 10,500-10,800 yuan/mt, while NORNICKEL premiums are 7,500 yuan/mt, flat for Jinchuan vs down but still supported by shrinking supply. Nickel briquette prices rose. Tight supply is expected to persist into next week, supporting nickel sentiment. | DR Congo plans to raise its stake in the Sicomines copper-and-cobalt venture with Chinese partners to 70% and seeks more infrastructure funding and compensation, arguing the 2008 deal was lopsided and priced minerals below market. Renegotiations are expected to start after President Tshisekedi returns from China. | DR Congo plans to raise its stake in the Sicomines copper-and-cobalt JV with Chinese partners from 32% to 70% and seek greater management control, citing limited gains under the 2008 infrastructure-for-minerals deal. Reuters reports groundwork for renegotiations ahead of discussions, potentially affecting copper supply expectations.

SMM reports that Nornickel nickel premiums rose as local supply tightened, with most NPI factories pausing quotations. However, trader stocks increased due to imported spot inflows tied to long-term orders. Spot prices for warrants were below market sources as futures fell and mills reduced shipments, driving down inventory. Overall, imported inflows may pressure nickel lower. | Crude oil prices rose as expectations shifted on OPEC+ production-cut signals, while the U.S. dollar weakened. Reflecting this risk-on macro backdrop, SHFE and LME base metals increased broadly: SHFE aluminium +3.38%, copper +2.17%, nickel +1.27%; LME aluminium +0.85%, copper +1.31%, nickel +1.29%. | SMM reported that spot nickel premiums fell at month-end on May 26. Jinchuan nickel premiums averaged 10,050 yuan/mt, down 600 yuan/mt, while NORNICKEL averaged 6,900 yuan/mt, also down 600 yuan/mt. Nickel briquette prices rose slightly, but transactions were slack amid continued premiums versus nickel sulphate. | Lifezone Metals has started offtake marketing for products from its Kabanga nickel project in Tanzania, targeting EV companies for nickel, cobalt and copper output. The feasibility work continues and no deal is agreed, but progress toward commercialisation and processing plans supports bullish near-term sentiment for nickel. | China’s refined nickel imports fell to 3,204 tonnes in April, the lowest since 2004, with imports from Indonesia and Russia also dropping sharply. Chinese stainless steel mills are shifting toward nickel pig iron (NPI) rather than Class I refined nickel, and EV battery growth may further reduce demand for high-purity nickel. | G7 expanded sanctions on Russia, reducing Russian nickel exports to markets like the Netherlands and the US while shifting flows to other buyers (notably Canada and China). At the same time, China’s electrowinning nickel capacity is set to rise sharply, increasing competition and oversupply risk, which the article expects to keep nickel prices pressured lower. | NickelSearch Ltd says drilling at Carlingup’s high-priority B1 target in Western Australia has confirmed and extended historical nickel sulphide results, making B1 a second target for immediate follow-up. Additional assays are expected, with diamond drilling planned for early June, but no production timing is provided.

SMM reports mixed LME/SHFE performance after a U.S. debt-ceiling agreement boosted risk appetite and weakened the dollar, but inflation and expected Fed hikes limit upside. Copper rose slightly yet sentiment may fade amid weaker consumption. Aluminium fell with inventories dropping but fundamentals seen unchanged. Nickel weakened due to falling premiums, rising stocks, and softer demand. | Indonesia, the world’s top nickel producer, is putting plans for a nickel export levy on hold while it develops a nickel price index to reduce volatility linked to LME benchmarks. The index will feed into royalties and the levy calculation. Export tax delay likely supports nickel sentiment near term amid LME uncertainty. | Indonesia is considering creating a nickel price index this year and has paused plans to tax nickel product exports, a senior government official said. Because Indonesia is the world’s biggest nickel producer, these policy and pricing benchmark signals could influence near-term nickel market expectations and support prices. | Business groups and officials say northern Minnesota/Wisconsin and Michigan’s Upper Peninsula could boost domestic production of critical minerals for EVs, especially nickel. The article cites the Eagle Mine’s nickel/copper output and Tamarack’s nickel supply agreement with Tesla, alongside USGS mapping funding and supportive federal policies. | Copper fell after the U.S. dollar hit a two-month peak, making dollar-priced metal more expensive for non-USD holders. Risk sentiment improved temporarily, but the stronger dollar kept pressure on the non-ferrous complex. LME nickel and aluminium also declined, suggesting broad near-term bearish momentum for industrial metals. | Indonesia’s government plans to launch a new nickel price index this year, separate from the current HPM benchmark. It also signaled a deferral of proposed export duties on NPI and high-grade nickel matte, rather than immediate implementation, potentially supporting near-term nickel supply/export flows and weighing on prices. | Indonesia said it plans to launch a new nickel price index this year, distinct from the current HPM benchmark for Indonesian nickel ore. The government also indicated it will defer planned export duties on nickel products such as NPI and high-grade nickel matte, delaying cost pressures and potentially supporting nickel prices. | Copper prices stayed near a six-month low as investors prepared for Chinese data expected to show continued factory contraction. Demand concerns were reinforced by weak construction activity, low Yangshan import premiums, and LME stocks rising to about 100,000 tonnes. Aluminium and nickel also fell as broader metals sentiment deteriorated. | Indonesia’s export-ban policy on nickel ores, smelter buildout, and potential further restrictions (bauxite ban; possible heavy tax if WTO appeal fails) are positioned to increase processed nickel output while potentially tightening effective supply. Rising EV battery demand is cited, supporting a bullish near-term nickel outlook. | NewRange Copper Nickel began an ~$18M salvage and recycle program at the former LTV Steel site to restore electrical systems and refurbish infrastructure for the NorthMet clean-energy critical minerals project. The work will salvage steel and concrete to support safer construction readiness. It reinforces ongoing development of copper and nickel resources. | Outokumpu said it will invest EUR 11 million for a 9.9% stake in Canada’s FPX Nickel to strengthen its sustainable nickel supply chain. The Baptiste project is planned to start producing near decade-end, targeting low-carbon nickel units suited for stainless steel feedstock. | The Market Herald released interviews and highlights corporate news, including FPX Nickel closing a private placement with Outokumpu as a strategic cornerstone investor. The financing is a positive signal for FPX’s nickel-related activities and can modestly improve short-term sentiment toward nickel prices, though it lacks operational detail.

Indonesian copper miner Amman Mineral’s $880m IPO will finance debt repayment and projects, including completing a copper smelter in Sumbawa with planned cathode output. The Indonesian government’s temporary raw export exemption supports revenue needed to finish the project, reinforcing near-term copper sentiment. | White & Case highlights Indonesia’s strong Q1 deal activity, including Nickel Industries’ March equity placement to fund purchases and raise its stakes in Indonesian nickel projects (including Huayue Nickel Cobalt). It frames the strategy as shifting toward battery-grade/Class 1 nickel for EV demand, which is supportive for nickel sentiment. | Copper prices slid after China’s manufacturing PMI came in weaker than expected, below 50 indicating contraction, reinforcing concerns about demand in the world’s top consumer. Copper was set for a second monthly loss and fell 5.8% so far this month. LME aluminium and nickel also declined alongside risk sentiment. | Macquarie’s Jim Lennon argues Indonesia will supply most of global nickel growth through 2029, and that strong supply versus slowing demand created a nickel surplus in 2022. With underutilized capacity, the market could stay surplus, weighing on LME nickel. Battery demand is growing but supply effects dominate near-term. | Fathom Nickel closed the first tranche of an upsized private placement to fund exploration at the Albert Lake and Gochager Lake projects in Saskatchewan. Proceeds support Canadian flow-through mining expenditures and general corporate uses. Base metals mostly fell: SHFE nickel plunged 4.81%, SHFE copper was slightly down, and SHFE aluminium rose modestly. Spot premiums of Norilsk nickel fell sharply as large arrivals and a sell-off pressured the market. SMM says Indonesia has the world’s richest nickel ore reserves (25.4 million mt nickel content in 2021) and higher-grade lateritic ore than the Philippines. A U.S. debt-default deal, Senate debt-ceiling legislation, and expectations of no further Fed hikes weakened the dollar and lifted risk assets. Aluminium and nickel also rose.

Reuters reports copper fell on Monday due to demand worries, notably in top consumer China, and a stronger dollar. Copper later partially recovered as the dollar retreated. Firefighters responded to a smouldering fire at Aurizon Port Services at Carrington, where 10m-high piles of copper and lead concentrate began to self-combust. Crews cooled the material for about seven hours while staff removed the top layers. The fire did not damage buildings and was extinguished the same day. | Avebury Nickel Mine is trucking 4,500 tonnes of nickel concentrate to Burnie Port for loading on a vessel to China over Tuesday to Thursday. It discusses ramping up production while noting nickel prices are below desired levels and the mine is near break-even. | Copper prices in Shanghai and London rose toward a four-week high as investors priced in potential Chinese stimulus and supportive policy for property, while LME on-warrant copper inventories fell sharply. The permit setback increases uncertainty over future copper and nickel supply from NorthMet. | NewRange said the US Army Corps of Engineers revoked the Section 404 Clean Water Act permit for the NorthMet Project, prompting it to review options and re-engage regulators.
