New Caledonia’s nickel industry is under crisis: Glencore is withdrawing from Koniambo and the site has shifted to “care and maintenance,” while Eramet’s SLN needed a French government loan to avoid bankruptcy. A proposed “nickel pact” aims at competitiveness as Eramet warns SLN could be wiped out within five years. | The article’s headline says Indonesia will keep nickel supply sufficient to hold global prices down. If markets take this as evidence of sustained supply and limited upside risk, nickel could face near-term downward or at least limited upward pressure as buyers anticipate easier availability. | Amaroq Minerals announced a significant new Ni-Cu discovery at its Stendalen project in South Greenland, reporting over 140m of disseminated nickel-copper sulphides and potential for higher-grade massive sulphides. The company plans additional geophysics and up to three drill rigs in 2024, boosting exploration upside for nickel and copper. | Amaroq Minerals reported a new high-tenor nickel-copper discovery at its Stendalen project in South Greenland, with 140m of disseminated magmatic sulphides intersected in the first scout hole and plans to scale drilling in 2024. It remains an exploration update with no production or immediate supply change. | SMM reports nickel pig iron (8-12% grade) averaged 960 yuan/nickel point, flat vs the previous working day. Indonesia’s RKAB policy is said to have lifted nickel ore prices and production cut expectations may reduce ferronickel supply. Yet off-season stainless demand and weaker output keep demand soft, leaving ferronickel largely stable. | SMM reports that Indonesia has approved 145 million wet tons of nickel ore quota for 2024, but under the new multi-year approval system the approval pace has slowed. With expected 241 million wet tons demand, the approved quota covers about 60%, and further approvals are expected to increase supply coverage. | The article argues Indonesia is flooding the market with low-cost nickel, sustaining a global surplus and keeping prices under pressure. It describes closures or reviews of non-Indonesia supply (including Western Australia) and mentions Reuters’ view that China and Indonesia may cut output to limit losses. | EV Minerals reported assay results from surface sampling at Ontario’s Lunge Project tied to a proposed acquisition, including multiple grab samples above 1% nickel and several above 1% copper (to ~2.54% Cu). The company plans a 2024 baseline exploration program; results are not production or resource updates.

The article headline claims Indonesia floods are delivering “cheap nickel” to the market and triggering a wave of unprofitable mines. Increased near-term supply from nickel operations would tend to weigh on nickel prices over the coming days, unless demand offsets the added availability. | London nickel prices fell after easing worries about supply from Indonesia’s nickel output. Three-month nickel on the LME declined, reversing part of the prior rally as investors reassessed the balance of supply and demand. The report cites Indonesia’s resilience via margins and benefits to its battery industry. | BNN Bloomberg highlights a Macquarie warning that nickel may flip to a market deficit as Indonesia faces hurdles. The report implies reduced near-term supply availability from Indonesia, which would tighten fundamentals and likely add upward pressure to nickel prices over the coming days to weeks. | Indonesia’s deputy minister overseeing nickel processing said LME nickel is unlikely to rebound above $18,000/t and that the government will ensure full market supply to reduce EV manufacturers’ costs. He added nickel should stay above $15,000/t due to potential smelter cuts below that level, balancing downside. | The article focuses on why nickel prices have fallen, citing a supply glut (including growing Indonesian nickel inflows), weaker-than-expected demand amid an economic hangover, and investor uncertainty about EV nickel usage. It notes ongoing 2024 price declines and reduced financing for nickel projects, reinforcing near-term bearish sentiment. | Indonesia’s government official said Indonesian nickel will remain in demand regardless of EV battery chemistry, arguing nickel is used not only for NMC batteries but also stainless steel and other materials. The piece also reiterates Indonesia’s domestic processing push and its halt on unprocessed nickel-ore exports. Net near-term price effect seems limited. | Spruce Ridge Resources completed its acquisition giving it an 80% interest in Homeland Nickel, which holds the Cleopatra and Red Flat nickel laterite deposits in southwest Oregon. The company plans permitting and up to 70 near-surface drillholes to assess grade and laterite thickness, alongside milestone-based study and construction payments. | Macquarie cut its 2024 nickel surplus forecast to 36,000t from 125,000t, citing stronger Chinese demand and corrected NPI/ferro-nickel accounting. With Indonesian ore licence controls and planned supply curtailments, the bank expects LME nickel prices may have bottomed near $16,000/t and have upside potential. | EV Minerals has acquired the Baldwin and Lunge nickel projects in Sudbury, Ontario, assuming a 2% NSR and gaining the right to buy back 1% for $1 million. The company also frames its broader EV Nickel Project as nickel-copper-cobalt. The announcement is sentiment-supportive but lacks near-term production impact.

The report says Chinese restocking is underway and frames it as supportive for battery mineral producers, with battery-related commodity prices turning higher. It notes several base- and battery-miner company share gains, including Nickel Industries, implying improved near-term sentiment for nickel prices. | SMM reports nickel prices rose due to slower approval of Indonesian nickel ore mining quotas under RKAB, raising ongoing concerns about ore supply despite a 2024 quota of 145 million wmt. Spot trading is subdued, downstream purchasing is limited by high prices, but smelters ramp output, and nickel is expected to trend slightly higher this week. | Western nickel producers face losses and mine closures as nickel prices have fallen sharply to around US$17,500/t. The article attributes the decline to expanding China- and Indonesia-controlled supply, including NPI exports for EV batteries. While ESG/tariff actions are proposed, near-term messaging stresses continued glut and weak prices. | SMM reports Shanghai nickel futures fell on March 4, alongside decreasing open interest and stop-loss/long liquidation signals. Fundamentally, the decline is attributed to a significant increase in global nickel inventories last week, which implies higher supply and continued downward pressure on nickel prices. | SMM reports Indonesia approved a large 2024 nickel ore quota, expected to accelerate, though ore supply concerns remain. Spot activity was muted as high nickel prices reduced downstream buying and lowered nickel plate demand. Traders cut premiums to sell. Despite bearish fundamentals, macro remains supportive and nickel is expected to trend slightly higher. | SMM data shows nickel inventories in Shanghai’s bonded zone rose slightly to 4,260 mt and pure nickel social inventories across six regions increased to 27,461 mt. The SHFE nickel price rose last week, but downstream demand was cautious due to high prices, leading to reduced buying and expectations of modest further inventory increases. | Macquarie nickel veteran Jim Lennon says the prior year’s nickel turmoil may end sooner than expected. He cites a major forecast change after a China trip, with unexpectedly strong Chinese demand and potentially slower Indonesian growth rebalancing the market after prices fell nearly 50%.

BHP says global decarbonisation pathways require major increases in critical minerals and forecasts tightening copper markets. It updated short-term balances to a refined copper deficit and very tight concentrate supply, citing Cobre Panama closure, other shortfalls, and new Chinese smelting. Fitch/BMI also supports higher copper prices amid supply constraints. | The Jakarta Post editorial claims Indonesia’s nickel dominance and downstreaming have quickly increased smelter output, contributing to a global nickel supply glut and pushing prices far below prior highs. It cites Indonesia’s rising share of global supply, discusses losses and potential closures abroad, and warns reserves may deplete before EV battery demand grows. | Investors are becoming more optimistic about nickel after last year’s rout. Macquarie’s nickel strategist Jim Lennon says unexpectedly strong Chinese demand and the possibility of slower Indonesian growth may help rebalance the market, potentially reducing bearish sentiment and supporting prices over the coming weeks. | SMM reports strong cost-side support for nickel sulphate prices. MHP prices are rising as overseas projects face economics that could reduce or stop production (e.g., Goro, First Quantum). Huafei is not at full output and circulating MHP supply is tight, while nickel salt and refined producers compete for feedstock. | Shanghai SMM reports battery-grade nickel sulphate prices rising to about 28,517 yuan/mt as supply remains tight. Nickel salt factories are restarting slowly amid scarce, expensive raw materials and low inventories. Demand from ternary cathode/precursor chains is active, supporting further price increases this week. | First Quantum says multiple parties want to buy a minority stake in its Zambian copper operations, after the company closed its Panama copper mine and needs balance-sheet support. It expects due diligence via a data room. The Zambian business includes Kansanshi/Sentinel copper and a nickel ramp-up. | Premium Nickel Resources reports additional assay results from underground drilling at its Selebi North Ni-Cu-Co mine in Botswana, extending massive sulphide mineralization down plunge. Highlights include intersections with ~2.25% NiEq and underlying nickel and copper grades. The release is exploration-focused, with limited immediate market impact on nickel/copper prices. | The London Metal Exchange rejected an Australian (with US backing) bid to recognize “clean” nickel. The proposal aimed to differentiate Australian nickel from “dirty nickel” amid a China-linked supply glut from Indonesia. The rejection reduces the chance of a clean-nickel premium, worsening near-term sentiment for nickel prices. | Goldman Sachs cautioned that the rebound in nickel prices is not the end of the battery metals bear market. The rally may reflect traders covering shorts, and analysts expect nickel prices to revert to a sell-off, potentially dropping as much as 25% over the near term.

Copper prices extended their decline as a steady U.S. dollar reduced demand appeal and traders were disappointed by China’s lack of new policy support at parliament. Analysts noted limited signs of construction ramp-up. The article also shows LME aluminium and nickel falling alongside copper, reinforcing bearish sentiment. | Nickel fell on expectations of better supply from Indonesia after approvals for mining production quotas from over 120 mineral companies, reducing fears of ore shortages from delayed approvals. Sentiment was further pressured by steady dollar strength and weaker China stainless-steel prices amid slow demand and limited policy measures. | Eramet reached an agreement with the French government to convert €320 million of loans to its loss-making New Caledonia nickel unit SLN, plus intra-group debt, into a quasi-equity instrument. This removes debt payments and reinforces SLN’s balance sheet, supporting continuity of nickel processing as negotiations continue. | SMM says battery-grade nickel sulfate prices rose March 6 amid tight supply: recycling-based salt plants resumed slowly due to strong waste material profits, inventories stayed low, and downstream replenishment demand continued. Rising MHP costs tied to LME nickel (81 coefficient level) caused losses for some plants, reinforcing strong near-term price pressure. | Reuters reports copper rose on a weaker dollar and falling LME exchange stocks, but gains were capped by concerns over insufficient China policy stimulus and weak construction demand. Nickel fell as supply prospects improved from Indonesia and stainless steel prices in China weakened. Aluminium edged up slightly. | Copper rose on a weaker dollar and continued declines in LME-registered exchange stocks, though gains were capped by concerns over lack of China stimulus and weak housing demand. Nickel fell as supply prospects improved from Indonesia and China stainless steel prices trended down. Aluminium was slightly higher. | The LME discussed growing calls for reforms and potential low-carbon (“green”) nickel pricing mechanisms. It says fast-tracked eligible brands have improved deliverable liquidity and price convergence, while producers question whether ESG-linked definitions can be standardized. The LME is not planning contract changes and will publish transaction/value data on low-carbon class 1 nickel. | Indonesia has approved a quota of 145 million wet tons of nickel ore for the next three years under a new multi-year approval system. The quota covers about 60% of expected 2024 ore demand, with further approvals expected to rise, improving nickel ore availability prospects. | Marvel Discovery completed drilling at its Duhamel Ni-Cu-Co (and Ti-V-Cr) property north of Quebec City, reporting multiple disseminated and massive sulfide intercepts including chalcopyrite. The release anticipates assays and further geophysics but provides no confirmed resource estimate or production timeline, limiting near-term market impact for copper and nickel. | Marvel Discovery reports successful drilling and multiple sulfide mineralization intersections at its Duhamel Ni-Cu-Co project in Quebec, including chalcopyrite-rich mineralization and historic nickel/copper grade highlights. The release is exploration-focused and does not indicate production, so any effect on short-term nickel or copper prices is likely negligible. | Marvel completed Phase 1 drilling at its Duhamel Ni-Cu-Co (and Ti-V-Cr) project north of Quebec City, reporting multiple sulfide intersections and chalcopyrite/pyrrhotite intervals. The company plans further geophysics while waiting for assays. This is exploration news; it is not tied to immediate market supply changes. | Marvel Discovery completed Phase 1 drilling (5 holes, 1,218m) at the Duhamel Ni-Cu-Co and Ti-V-Cr project in Quebec, intersecting multiple sulfide-rich intervals, including chalcopyrite-bearing mineralization. Results are promising for nickel and copper, but no resource estimate or production schedule is provided. | Marvel Discovery Corp. reported completion of Phase 1 drilling at the Duhamel Ni-Cu-Co project in Quebec, intersecting multiple sulfide-rich intervals including disseminated and massive zones. Chalcopyrite and other mineralization were observed, suggesting potential for nickel and copper. The update is exploratory with no immediate supply figures.

France agreed to convert €320m of prior loans into debt “quasi-equity” for Eramet’s SLN and plans further aid tied to reforms to lower nickel production costs. Koniambo is in care-and-maintenance and Prony gets a €140m loan, alongside an emergency jobs plan—reducing near-term supply risk for nickel. | Singapore’s MAS charged former Envysion Wealth/Hui Xun Asset Management executives in connection with a cited billion-dollar nickel investment fraud case involving alleged false promises of nickel purchases. The allegations center on investor money raised for nickel trading/returns, but no concrete data is provided on real nickel flows affecting near-term prices. | Global nickel producers including BHP and Andrew Forrest are urging the LME to introduce a green price premium and formally distinguish “dirty” from sustainably produced (“green”) nickel. Indonesia’s low-cost supply has driven a slump, while PT CNGR’s bid for a ‘good delivery brand’ could reshape nickel pricing via ESG-linked differentials on the LME. | Granite Creek Copper announced a non-brokered private placement raising about $1.5 million to fund general corporate purposes and advancement of strategic initiatives. The company focuses on copper projects in Yukon and also holds a Star copper-nickel-PGM project. The financing is not tied to near-term production changes or supply disruption. | France will convert and provide financing tied to a March “nickel pact” to rescue New Caledonia’s struggling nickel producers, including Eramet’s SLN and loans for Prony. Koniambo production has stopped, but aid and cost-reduction reforms aim to prevent further disruption amid tough Indonesian/Chinese competition.

Canada says Western countries may consider tariffs on nickel from Indonesia and China over concerns about market manipulation. Indonesia’s share of nickel supply has surged, contributing to a major global oversupply and an 80% price collapse. Producers face mine closures and surplus pressure, implying near-term bearish sentiment for nickel. | The Pentagon discussed with Australia’s resources minister options to co-invest with the Australian government in an Australian nickel project after a collapse in nickel prices. The decline has triggered closures and write-downs of projects, reflecting a glut. Potential funding support could ease downside sentiment for nickel. | The LME’s rejection of a separate index for “green premium” nickel is described as a setback for Australian producers such as BHP and Wyloo Metals. Without a differentiated pricing mechanism, their ability to earn higher premiums over Indonesian nickel suppliers is weakened, reinforcing broader bearish sentiment after nickel’s price collapse. | Experts argue Indonesia may need to adjust its nickel downstream strategy as EV makers increasingly consider lithium iron phosphate (LFP) batteries, which use iron cathodes rather than nickel. The shift is linked to nickel price fluctuations, with forecasts of LFP gaining market share by 2025. | Experts say Indonesia may need to adjust its nickel downstream strategy as EV makers consider alternatives. The report cites expectations that momentum will build behind lithium iron phosphate (LFP) batteries rather than nickel-based chemistries, potentially weakening nickel demand outlook and weighing on prices. | Australia’s Resources Minister met US Defence officials to discuss co-investing in an Australian nickel project amid weakening nickel prices that have forced some operations into reduced output or care and maintenance. The talks aim to preserve the nickel supply chain, alongside prior discussions on investment incentives and infrastructure support. | SMM reports China’s February refined nickel production at 24,600 tonnes, down ~3% from January due to fewer Spring Festival working days but up 42.2% year-on-year. March output is forecast to rise to ~25,000 tonnes, though higher raw-material prices may constrain some outsourced production. | EV Nickel upsized its previously announced private placement of flow-through shares to fund Canadian exploration expenses for its Shaw Dome nickel project in Ontario. A strategic investor increased participation to maintain its pro-rata stake. The announcement supports sentiment toward the company’s nickel resource pipeline but not immediate supply volumes.

Core Nickel announces mobilization and start of its 2024 winter drill program (~1,000 metres) at the Halfway Lake nickel project in Manitoba. Targets focus on the historical W62 zone and nearby magnetic anomalies. The work is fully funded using a Manitoba Mineral Development Fund grant, supporting exploration continuity and nickel upside sentiment. | Copper steadied ahead of Chinese loan data and upcoming U.S. inflation figures, with traders watching for demand signals and for earlier Fed rate cuts that could weaken the dollar. Support came from lower LME warehouse stocks. Nickel edged up on reduced short bets, and aluminium rose slightly. | Spruce Ridge Resources said it signed a binding letter of intent to acquire the Shamrock nickel sulphide property in Oregon. The project’s historic results report nickel and copper grades above 1.0%, with reported bulk flotation recoveries. The deal is a sentiment-positive step for future nickel and copper supply expectations. | Core Nickel announced mobilization for its 2024 winter drill program at the 100% owned Halfway Lake nickel project in Manitoba. The ~1,000m drilling targets the historical W62 zone with previously reported nickel mineralized intercepts, supported by MMDF grant funding. It is a positive development for nickel exploration sentiment. | SMM reports March 11 high-nickel pig iron at 956.5 yuan/nickel point, unchanged day-on-day. Indonesian smelter production fell 10–20% due to nickel ore shortages. Stainless steel demand is sluggish and policy effects from RKAB approvals are fading, leading expectations of limited upward room for ferronickel prices. | SMM reports that on March 11 nickel premiums were mixed: Jinchuan premium averaged 1350 yuan/ton and Russian nickel premium averaged -250 yuan/ton. Trading in the spot market was weak, with deals concentrated during a downward price move and downstream mostly doing rigid replenishment. Nickel bean prices rose slightly day-on-day. | The article says nickel prices are sliding due to market oversupply, especially rising Indonesian supply, alongside weakening EV demand. It notes nickel’s role in EV batteries and references falling EV sales, reduced subsidies, and destocking by Chinese battery raw-material firms, all weighing on sentiment and pricing into 2024.

Aluminium prices rose toward six-week highs as traders priced in seasonally stronger demand, with LME three-month aluminium around $2,270/t. However, China’s ample supply and rising inventories could limit upside. A firmer U.S. dollar after inflation data pressured industrial metals, though nickel was slightly higher. | Spot copper concentrate TC/RCs in cif Asia-Pacific plunged to $12.70/t in early March from $19.80/t a week earlier, the lowest since tracking began. Sources link the decline to unexpected mine supply disruptions (e.g., Cobre Panama) and persistent concentrate deficits, squeezing smelter feedstock economics and raising risks of cuts. | Indonesia’s tech and fintech M&A is highlighted, but the piece also notes active deals in critical minerals. Antam sold stakes to CATL and Australia’s Nickel Industries bought into an Indonesian nickel project, reinforcing ongoing investor focus on Indonesia’s nickel supply for EV battery demand.

Indonesia’s next president, Prabowo, vows to maintain downstreaming by expanding domestic nickel processing and potentially halting certain exports. While prior smelting expansion helped create a processed nickel oversupply and a 45% price fall last year, advisers are discussing supply-control steps like pausing new smelters to prevent further declines. | DLP Resources announced early rock-sampling results from its 100% owned Esperanza porphyry copper-molybdenum project in southern Peru, with multiple samples returning ~1.1%–4.7% Cu and associated Mo and other elements. The update may modestly support copper sentiment but is exploration-stage with no immediate supply impact. | Copper jumped to a seven-month high after China’s smelters (half of global mined copper processing) agreed on a symbolic joint production cut, without specifying volumes. SHFE and LME copper futures surged on the announcement. Rising concentrate competition and low smelter incomes reinforced supply tightness expectations. | Fathom Nickel announced completion of drilling at its Albert Lake project and mobilization to the Gochager Lake project, where it plans 2,000–2,500 meters starting March 12. The program targets off-hole conductivity linked to historic nickel-copper-cobalt mineralization, but assays are expected later. | SMM commentary says nickel has risen in recent days, with Shanghai and London prices printing fresh highs. Support comes from cost dynamics—nickel sulfate/raw materials are tight while refined output may be loose—and from macro tailwinds like Chinese policy optimism and a weakening US dollar as rate hikes near their end. | SMM reports March 13 battery-grade nickel sulfate prices rising: the index reached 29,567 yuan/ton (+49 yuan/ton day-on-day) and the range was 29,400–29,700 yuan/ton. Demand for downstream replenishment persists, supply-demand remains tight, and elevated raw material prices keep many salt plants operating at losses, supporting further upside. | Arya Resources reported assay results from eight holes in its Dunlop Ni-Cu project in Saskatchewan, including intervals averaging ~0.32–0.35% copper and ~0.39–0.61% nickel, alongside cobalt. The release is exploratory and does not indicate near-term production changes, limiting immediate price impact on copper and nickel. | Arya Resources reported assay results from fall 2023 drilling at its Dunlop Ni-Cu project in northern Saskatchewan, including intervals grading about 0.32–0.35% copper and 0.39–0.61% nickel over tens of meters. The release is exploratory and does not indicate near-term production or supply disruptions. | Ranchero Gold has completed an option agreement to acquire a full interest in Recharge Resources’ Pinchi Lake Nickel Project in British Columbia. The deal involves issuing shares, a hold period pending TSX approval, staged cash payments over years, and exploration funding milestones through 2026. Nickel potential remains exploration-based. | SMM data shows nickel stocks in Shanghai bonded zone rose slightly to 4,460 mt, while pure nickel social inventory across six Chinese regions increased to 27,607 mt. High nickel prices made downstream buyers cautious and slowed procurement, so inventories are expected to accumulate slightly this week. | Endeavour Financial has sued Horizonte Minerals in the UK over disputed nickel-project “success fees,” but the broader issue is Horizonte’s Araguaia ferronickel project funding strain. Costs reportedly surged 87% to over US$1bn and first metal was pushed to early 2026, increasing risk of delayed nickel supply amid weak prices. | Australia’s nickel industry is under pressure as cheap Indonesian supply floods global markets and prices fall below $20,000/tonne. The federal government is preparing a support package and urges EV makers to pay a “clean, green premium” for lower-carbon, higher-ESG Australian nickel, potentially supporting sentiment.

Stantec was selected to provide environmental consulting for Canada Nickel’s Crawford Nickel Project in Ontario, including preparing the Impact Statement and related studies under Canada’s Impact Assessment Act. The mine is expected to produce about 50,000 tons of nickel annually, but the news is about approvals, not immediate supply changes. | Australia’s Avebury Nickel Mine near Zeehan is preparing to re-enter care and maintenance, with many workers told they will not return. The shutdown is attributed to a 50% decline in global nickel prices, blamed on cheap low-quality Indonesian imports, reinforcing bearish sentiment for near-term nickel. | LME nickel has surged from below $16,000/t to above $18,000/t as traders increased buying to cover large short positions. The move was supported by higher Chinese nickel sulphate prices, delays to Indonesian ore licences, and class 2 supply cuts, though class 1 oversupply keeps downside risk.

EV Minerals reports additional historical drill results and database capture for its EV Nickel-Copper-Cobalt project in Quebec, highlighting near-surface nickel and copper mineralization and planning additional drilling as assays return. The update is supportive for long-run resource potential, but it does not imply immediate supply changes for nickel or copper. | A pro-independence group in New Caledonia proposes an OPEC-like nickel producers’ forum to stabilize prices. Meanwhile, New Caledonia’s main nickel operations face severe disruption: Koniambo is in non-production sleep mode and other producers face bankruptcy risk, as low-cost Indonesia/China output pressures prices. French aid is conditional on reforms and a “nickel pact.” | A pro-independence group in New Caledonia proposes an OPEC-like nickel producers organisation to support stable prices. Meanwhile New Caledonia’s nickel industry faces shutdown risk, including Koniambo (KNS) put in non-production sleep mode and aid conditioned on signing a “nickel pact.” | The article explains Indonesia’s central role in global nickel supply and how growth in mining and smelting has reshaped nickel markets. It highlights controversies around production practices and a deadly smelter fire, both of which could disrupt supply. It also notes potential EV shifts away from nickel batteries, tempering price impact. | Shanghai copper jumped to a near three-year high and LME copper to a 10-month peak as concerns grew that China—top refined copper producer—will lower smelter operating rates, adjust maintenance, and postpone new projects. Aluminium and nickel were also higher, helped by supportive supply factors. | A pro-independence group proposes an OPEC-like nickel producers organisation to help stabilize nickel prices. The plan coincides with New Caledonia’s nickel crisis: Koniambo placed in non-production sleep mode, SLN and Prony facing bankruptcy, and French bailouts conditional on reforms under a “nickel pact” to cut costs and adjust markets. | The webinar summarizes the global nickel market, highlighting growing NPI capacity in Indonesia, a likely (but possibly smaller) NPI surplus, and production slowdowns or postponements. It also discusses demand growth drivers for nickel sulfate and refined nickel tied to stainless and batteries, offsetting surplus pressures. | Alaska Energy Metals filed an updated NI 43-101 technical report for its Eureka deposit in Alaska, combining Eureka West and East and upgrading part of the resource to indicated. The company reports higher in-situ nickel and associated copper, and plans further drilling in 2024. | Alaska Energy Metals filed an updated NI 43-101 technical report for its Eureka deposit at the Nikolai nickel project in Alaska. The update connects deposits and upgrades part of the resource to indicated, reporting sizable nickel and copper contained metal. Investors may view this as supportive for near-term nickel and some copper sentiment. | Alaska Energy Metals filed an updated NI 43-101 technical report for its Eureka nickel project, upgrading and expanding the deposit and reporting Indicated resources of 813 Mt at 0.29% NiEq plus associated contained nickel and copper. It also notes metallurgical work for ferrochrome, but no near-term production changes. | Fathom Nickel highlights plans to develop two nickel projects (Albert Lake and Gochager Lake) with high-grade nickel sulfide aimed at EV battery demand. It cites expected EV sales growth and nickel’s growing share in batteries. The news may modestly support nickel sentiment but provides limited near-term supply detail.

Mineral Resources (Chris Ellison) will acquire Poseidon Nickel’s Lake Johnston WA concentrator and tenements for $15 million, plus a royalty stream. The plan is to retrofit the nickel infrastructure for lithium processing under a lithium strategy, implying reduced nickel processing capacity in Western Australia. | Copper prices were mixed as tight supply concerns outweighed demand uncertainty. LME copper eased slightly while SHFE copper hit record highs on expectations of potential smelter output cuts in China. China’s industrial output and retail sales rose, but real estate remained weak. Aluminium and nickel also drifted lower. | London copper eased 0.5% while Shanghai copper reached fresh highs on expectations of potential smelter output cuts in China, but analysts cautioned that consumption is slow and inventories keep rising. The report also shows softer LME aluminium and nickel, while SHFE aluminium rose and nickel fell. | MinRes signed an agreement to acquire Poseidon Nickel’s Lake Johnston nickel concentrator plant in Western Australia, paying consideration on execution and completion. MinRes plans to convert the facility to process lithium ores for its Goldfields operations, though the asset is initially a nickel concentrator, affecting near-term nickel supply expectations only modestly. | Indonesia’s investment ministry says Vale Indonesia is exploring a new $1.9 billion HPAL project in Sulawesi, targeting 60,000 tons of nickel in MHP annually for EV batteries. The plan would be Vale’s third HPAL project, alongside existing Pomalaa and Sorowako builds, indicating potential future nickel supply growth. | Copper steadied after a rally, supported by improving Chinese macro data and a softer U.S. dollar. A rare agreement by China’s smelters to cut output boosted sentiment and helped prices break key technical levels. Copper trades near $9,061. Nickel fell on higher LME warehouse stocks; aluminium was slightly lower. | Canada Nickel reports positive initial assays from 2024 drilling: at Newmarket, two holes intersect nickel mineralization (e.g., 373m of 0.24% Ni), and at the Reid property, a best interval to date (675m of 0.25% Ni including higher grades). More drilling is planned and a resource target is outlined. | SMM reports Shanghai nickel main contract fell to 139,950 yuan/ton, down 1,920 yuan/ton from the prior close, with higher volume and open interest, shorts adding more positions. Spot transactions remain weak, though some downstream purchased after afternoon declines, leading SMM to expect continued price fluctuations. | Black mass market participants observe higher nickel (SHFE/LME) alongside a lithium price uptick, but say downstream demand for black mass remains weak and oversupply persists. Recyclers report limited ability to raise operating rates, and nickel black mass payables are largely unchanged week on week, with buyers still negotiating quality-based copper/aluminium thresholds. | BHP cut about a quarter of contractors at its West Musgrave nickel and copper project and is reviewing whether to pause its Australian nickel division amid a severe nickel price slump and operational losses. The move follows warnings of difficulty in the nickel sector as supply surges pressured prices. | Indonesia’s investment ministry says PT Vale Indonesia is exploring a new US$1.9-billion HPAL plant on Sulawesi, intended to produce nickel MHP for EV batteries. The facility would be Vale’s third HPAL project, with potential 60,000 t/y output. Existing Vale HPAL projects and MIND ID’s stake support nickel-focused sentiment. | SMM reports battery-grade nickel sulphate prices rising on tight supply, with slow recovery in hydrometallurgical recycling and active restocking supported by better vehicle sales. MHP supply shortages and elevated nickel prices increase raw material costs, widening nickel salt producers’ losses. Outlook remains bullish but upside may be limited. | The US Department of Defense granted Doe Run Resources $7m under the Defense Production Act to complete a demonstration-scale hydrometallurgical plant in Missouri to improve extraction and separation of nickel (and cobalt). The project supports domestic processing and supply-chain resilience for defense demand, which can offer near-term nickel price support.

Indonesia’s mining ministry has issued nickel ore production quotas totaling 152.62 million metric tons so far this year, alongside tin quotas. The government has approved production for 191 miners and is still processing more than 500 proposals, indicating ongoing supply management in a key nickel-producing country. | Copper fell on the LME as investors took profits after a rally and the dollar strengthened ahead of the Fed decision. The article cites weaker near-term China buying intentions. It also notes losses in nickel and aluminium, reflecting broader base-metal sentiment plus easing supply disruption signals from Indonesia. | Shanghai nickel fell during the March 19 session, with declining volume and open interest suggesting longer positions were closing. The key catalyst was Indonesia’s completion of nickel ore quota approvals for 107 companies, easing expectations of tight ore supply, plus signs intermediate production tightness may ease in April. Nickel is expected to stay weak. | Canadian North Resources reported a 172% increase in NI 43-101 indicated mineral resources at its Ferguson Lake project, including copper (66.1 Mt, 1,093 Mlb) and nickel (678 Mlb), with cobalt and PGM values also stated. It signals exploration potential, but provides no immediate production or supply disruption for prices. | BHP cut roughly a quarter of contractor staff at its West Musgrave nickel and copper project in Western Australia, citing the impacts of a global nickel price crisis. The company is also reviewing capital spend and business plans after shutting Nickel West, amid losses tied to nickel oversupply and a weaker outlook. | Shanghai Non ferrous Metals reports Ni 1.5% ore prices rebounded to about $46/wmt CIF, up $0.6. The rise is linked to Surigao’s rainy season reducing Philippines-to-China exports, lowering spot transactions, and coinciding with lower Chinese port inventories and weaker weekly arrivals. | BHP has reduced contractors and deferred a 1000-bed camp plan linked to rebuilding infrastructure at its Kalgoorlie nickel smelter, with ageing assets at risk of being mothballed. The company seeks to clarify the outlook for its loss-making nickel division, creating near-term uncertainty for nickel supply.

The Financial Review brief reports that BHP is cutting contractors at its Kalgoorlie nickel smelter. Alongside other unrelated items, this personnel change is the only element directly tied to industrial metals, potentially indicating disrupted or reduced nickel production capacity in the near term. | Shanghai nickel prices fell about 2% as fears of tight supply from Indonesia eased. A mining ministry official said Indonesia is accelerating the approval process for issuing mining production quotas, with progress already reported on nickel ore. Tin also declined, while LME nickel showed a smaller rebound. | Susan McDonald urges adding copper to Australia’s critical minerals list to protect supply chains and unlock financing and international investment. She cites nickel disruption after delays to show potential job risks. The piece notes copper prices rising on mine/smelter supply concerns, while processing fees have fallen. | SMM analysis says nickel prices rose in February, supported by a weaker US dollar, favorable domestic policies, and tighter supply from slower Indonesian ore quota approvals. For March, sentiment on ore quotas is easing while demand recovers. However, rising inventories are expected to pressure nickel prices in late March. | Talon Metals reported drill results from hole 23TK0447 at its Tamarack project, outside the current resource area, including 12.7m grading 6.04% Ni and 2.68% Cu (7.5% NiEq) and additional disseminated sulphides. The company links its thesis to nickel’s recent price decline and plans more updates soon. | Talon Metals reported drill results from the Tamarack Nickel-Copper-Cobalt Project, including a 12.7m intercept assaying 6.04% Ni and 2.68% Cu (7.5% NiEq) outside the resource area, plus additional disseminated sulphides. Management cites the need for high-grade production amid falling nickel prices. | The article attributes the post-holiday rise in nickel sulfate prices to tighter supply-demand conditions in finished products versus raw materials. Wet intermediate supply shrank due to slower Indonesia ramp-ups and scrap availability remained weak, while downstream inventory drawdowns intensified the mismatch. Prices rose faster than raw materials and profits recovered, but further upside may be limited. | Indonesia, the dominant player in nickel supply, plans to keep LME nickel prices below $18,000/mt. The government moved quickly to address quota-approval delays that had raised deficit fears. Still, the stated pricing cap and low-cost production could pressure nickel toward lower levels despite a recent rebound.

China’s February nickel pig iron imports fell MoM, with Indonesia-linked volumes down 14–17% MoM, attributed to slow Indonesian nickel mine RKAB approvals and the Chinese New Year holiday. Despite YOY growth, the delay suggests near-term tighter nickel supply, while recovery is expected as approvals accelerate. | Yle reports that Russian nickel continues to move by rail into Finland and flows to the Nornickel Harjavalta smelter despite checkpoint closures, because nickel is not covered by EU sanctions. The report also notes that freight strikes have not halted these shipments, suggesting steady near-term nickel supply to the EU. | Indonesia’s government approved 152.62mn tonnes of nickel mining quotas for 2024 (RKAB). Industry sources cited limitations and said approved ore quotas appear far below 2024 demand and may not be fully mined. Given nickel ore demand projections and constraints on ore supply to smelters, near-term nickel availability looks tighter. | BHP reduced contractor headcount at its Kalgoorlie nickel smelter and halted a new precipitation system, citing the current rout in nickel prices and an industry surplus—especially from Indonesia and China. The firm is also assessing whether to deactivate nickel operations if prices do not improve. | Lifezone Metals says Tanzania issued a licence for its Kahama multi-metal processing facility, allowing refining of nickel and copper (and cobalt) from the Kabanga Nickel Project using hydromet technology. The refinery is planned within a Special Economic Zone with tax benefits, supporting improved in-country beneficiation prospects. | The article argues the nickel market is facing continued pressure as Indonesia’s nickel laterite mines increase output and add new supply. This supply influx is described as flooding the market, implying weaker near-term balance and downward price pressure for nickel.

EV Minerals reported historic drill targets and selected intercepts from its EV Nickel-Copper-Cobalt project in Quebec, including multi-metre grades of nickel and copper and plans for follow-up drilling after assay results. The update may modestly support market sentiment for nickel and copper supply potential, but it is exploratory with no immediate production impact. | EV Minerals reports compelling North Zone drill targets and historic assay intersections at its EV Nickel-Copper-Cobalt project in Quebec. Multiple intervals include >1% nickel and meaningful copper grades, supporting potential resource expansion via infill drilling. The company plans follow-up work and metallurgical evaluation, but no near-term production change is stated. | France set a March-end deadline to finalize an agreement to restore New Caledonia’s nickel industry, including €200m/year in subsidies for energy and investment in electricity production. The plan targets cost reductions versus Indonesian competition and seeks more European supply, easing bankruptcy/supply disruption risks. | Refined nickel imports stay depressed as Russian nickel inflows decline, with difficulties signing long-term Russian contracts. Indonesia’s supplies rise, but refined nickel exports fell in February due to Spring Festival logistics disruptions and increased nickel sulfate prices, which led some producers to prioritize sulfate output over refined nickel. | Archer Exploration reported winter 2024 drilling results from its Grasset nickel sulphide project in Quebec, including intersections of high nickel (e.g., 2.67% Ni) with some copper content. The company frames results as extending mineralization at depth but provides no production, shipment, or inventory changes. | Tanzania awarded Lifezone Metals licences to operate a multi-metal hydromet facility at its Kabanga project, including production of nickel and copper (and cobalt). The Kahama Refinery licence follows SEZ and mining licence steps, leveraging existing Buzwagi infrastructure and aiming for local production with lower transport and operating costs.

EcoPro Invests around US$11 million in a nickel smelting factory in Indonesia. The headline implies expansion of nickel processing capacity, which can shift expectations for near-term nickel supply availability. Such capacity-related developments typically influence nickel prices through sentiment about inventories and refined output. | EcoPro said it invested $11 million for a 9% stake in Indonesia’s Green Eco Nickel, a smelter processing 20,000 tons of nickel annually. The firm also cited an earlier $86 million nickel smelting investment in Morowali and plans more Indonesian nickel processing projects to secure supply for battery precursors. | London copper rebounded, up 0.5%, as China’s inventory build in SHFE-tracked warehouses slowed for the first time this week. SHFE inventories still remain high, but analysts cite signs of end-of-accumulation and potential Q2 smelter maintenance effects. LME aluminium and nickel also edged higher. | EcoPro invested $11m for a 9% stake in Indonesia’s Green Eco Nickel smelter, operated by GEM, to expand nickel supply for lithium-ion battery materials. The smelter produces about 20,000 tons/year. EcoPro plans to scale ownership and process nickel into nickel sulfate for precursors. | The piece reviews nickel supply concentration and notes nickel prices averaged about $22,942/mt in Q3 2023, 11.6% lower year-on-year. It attributes weakness to soft China battery-sector demand and rising supply, especially from Indonesia. It also forecasts further nickel price declines in 2023 and 2024 as supply grows. | SMM reports Shanghai nickel closed lower on March 25, with reduced trading volume and open interest suggesting position lightening and a mild downward bias. Fundamentals are viewed as weakening: nickel mine approval is progressing faster and Indonesian intermediate output is rising, easing expectations of tight raw-material supply. Nickel is expected to remain weak and volatile. | SMM reports that 8–12% high-nickel pig iron averaged 942.5 yuan per nickel point on March 25, down 2.5 yuan from the prior working day. Despite faster nickel ore quota approvals, nickel iron production remains low in March. Persistent weak stainless steel demand and resulting inventory build keep nickel iron prices under pressure. | EcoPro invested US$11m for a 9% stake in Indonesia’s Green Eco Nickel, a nickel smelting plant in Sulawesi with 20,000 tonnes/year capacity, aiming to secure refined nickel for its battery materials affiliate. It also plans further investments, building on a prior stake in another nickel refining facility. | Ivanhoe Electric completed its earn-in to secure a 60% interest in the Samapleu-Grata nickel-copper project in Ivory Coast. A 2024 PEA from Sama highlights potential 16-year open-pit production of copper and nickel concentrates with reported NPV/IRR, improving expected future supply economics for both metals. | Ivanhoe Electric completed its earn-in to hold 60% of the Samapleu-Grata nickel-copper project in Côte d’Ivoire. Sama’s 2024 preliminary economic assessment forecasts a 16-year open-pit producing annual copper concentrate and nickel concentrate, based on long-term copper and nickel price assumptions. The update is preliminary and not expected to shift supply soon. | Future Battery Minerals will acquire an 85% stake in the Coolgardie Nickel subsidiary holding the Miriam Project near its Kangaroo Hills lithium asset in Western Australia. The project is positioned as drill-ready for spodumene and contains high-grade lithium samples. Deal closing is mid-May 2024, subject to conditions. | Indonesia’s nickel ore prices rebounded as rainy-season conditions in Surigao reduced Philippine export volumes, limiting spot availability. Indonesian laterite ore grades rose and downstream smelters face thin inventories and higher purchasing costs. Chinese port nickel ore stocks fell WoW, indicating tighter near-term supply into late March. | Nickel prices fell last week, with SHFE ending at 134,710 yuan/mt (-5.05% WoW). The selloff is linked to faster Indonesia RKAB approvals easing fears of scarce supply, weak nickel sulphate transactions, and expectations of higher refined output alongside cautious downstream demand. | Ivanhoe Electric says it has completed its earn-in to acquire a 60% interest in the Samapleu-Grata nickel-copper project in Côte d'Ivoire after meeting the agreed expenditure requirements. The update relates to ownership of a future nickel and copper resource but does not indicate near-term output changes.

Antam, in partnership with CBL, plans to begin construction in 2025 of an RKEF nickel plant and an HPAL plant to produce EV battery feedstock in Indonesia. Feasibility studies are ongoing, including potential use of natural gas instead of coal. The move supports “green nickel” and expands processing capacity plans. | Copper hit its lowest in nearly two weeks as the Chinese yuan weakened, eroding China’s purchasing power for dollar-priced commodities. LME copper also faced pressure from higher LME-approved inventories. The move coincided with softer prices in nickel and aluminium-related signals, reinforcing bearish near-term base-metal sentiment. | Indonesia’s Antam plans to start construction in 2025 of two nickel processing facilities, including RKEF to make nickel matte and HPAL to extract battery materials from nickel ores. Feasibility work is ongoing, with Antam considering natural-gas power for HPAL, subject to economic viability. | Tartisan Nickel Corp. hired Northwest Solutions to complete design, permitting, and management for an all-season access road for its Kenbridge Nickel Project, alongside Indigenous community liaison efforts and internal finance/communications support. The release flags 2024 permitting and baseline-study work, but provides no production or supply-volume change for nickel. | Kenorland Minerals received a $300,000 Manitoba Mineral Development Fund grant to support exploration at its South Thompson Nickel Project, including a large-scale VTEM survey to identify drill targets. The release highlights nickel belt geology and historical results, but no near-term production impacts or supply disruptions. | Kenorland Minerals received a $300,000 Manitoba Mineral Development Fund grant to advance exploration at its South Thompson nickel project, including a large-scale VTEM electromagnetic survey to find drill targets. The announcement improves nickel exploration activity, but it is early-stage and unlikely to affect near-term nickel supply expectations.

SMM’s morning nickel comment reports SHFE nickel weakening and expects continued mild decline. It attributes softer raw-material tightness to faster nickel-ore approvals and ramping Indonesian intermediate output, while downstream spot buying recovered only because prices fell sharply. Overall, it forecasts weak nickel price fluctuations. | The article argues nickel prices are likely to drop further in 2024. It cites forecasts of a continued global surplus supported by higher Indonesian production and expanding China refining capacity, which previously drove nickel to multi-year lows. Potential output cuts are mentioned, but the overall market outlook remains bearish. | China’s February refined nickel output was 24,600 mt (-3% MoM, +42% YoY) with operating rates still high. March production is estimated to rise to 25,000 mt (+1.6% MoM), supported by sustained nickel price gains, though higher raw-material costs and weaker domestic spot demand limited feedstock availability. Import clearances declined.

Merdeka Copper Gold said it targets 14,000–16,000 metric tons of copper from the Wetar mine and 85,000–92,000 tons of nickel in nickel pig iron plus up to 55,000 tons of nickel in matte this year. These output forecasts are disclosed without any immediate disruption or policy change. | Copper prices rose as top Chinese smelters agreed on plans for a 5%-10% production cut due to tight raw-material supply. China’s industrial profit improvement and a stronger yuan fixing supported buyer purchasing power, though copper gains were partly capped by inventory build-ups. Aluminium and nickel also edged higher. | India is sending a delegation to Chile, a leading copper and lithium producer, to explore acquisitions of overseas assets to bolster its energy-transition supply. The move is part of India’s broader strategy to secure critical minerals abroad. The article does not specify deal volumes, limiting short-term price pressure. | EV Nickel Inc. announced it closed a $5.12 million flow-through financing, raising funds to develop its Shaw Dome nickel exploration assets in Ontario. The proceeds are earmarked for eligible Canadian exploration and “critical mineral” expenditures. The release does not indicate immediate production changes or supply disruption impacting near-term nickel prices.

Copper prices rose as China demand outlook improved and fears of tighter raw material supply, particularly copper concentrate, emerged. LME three-month copper climbed 1.1% and SHFE May copper rose as manufacturing activity strengthened. Aluminium and nickel also edged higher, while macro worries over Fed rate cuts weighed on yields. | SMM reports the SHFE nickel contract closed higher but spot trades weakened overall. It attributes soft sentiment to rising this-week nickel supply from increased overseas nickel plate arrivals and lower nickel-bean prices. Nickel sulfate demand inquiry is still moderate, while ferronickel remains weak due to deadlock demand. | The piece discusses the 2023 nickel downturn and profit impairments at miners/traders, but highlights signs of a bottoming process. It notes Shanghai nickel futures rising over 10% since late January and expects improving supply-demand, supported by stainless steel production growth and rising NEV/HPAL nickel sulfate demand.  | The piece discusses nickel prices staying low in 2023 amid global oversupply and Fed-driven dollar strength, citing large profit declines for miners and nickel producers. It notes a recent rise in Shanghai nickel futures since late January and argues for marginal bottoming support from improving demand (stainless steel, NEVs). | Harita Nickel (Trimegah Bangun Persada) reported Rp 5.61 trillion net profit in 2023, up 20.4% y/y, citing increased revenue, operational efficiency, and higher nickel processing and mining revenue. Management noted headwinds from falling nickel prices but highlighted production optimization on Obi Island. | Azimut and KGHM report 2023 exploration at the Kukamas Property, including a newly discovered amphibolite-hosted nickel-copper-PGE showing with up to 1.36% Ni and 0.12% Cu in grab samples. The target remains untested by drilling, and a partner-funded follow-up program is planned for 2024. | On April 1, the Shanghai nickel main contract opened at 131,190 yuan/ton and closed at 131,600 yuan/ton, up 680 yuan/ton (+0.91%). Trading volume was lower and open interest edged down, suggesting reduced positioning and a wait-and-see stance, while price ultimately finished higher after an early dip. | The article reports SMM high-nickel pig iron averaging 930 yuan per nickel point, slightly lower day-on-day, and expects ferronickel output to stay low due to nickel ore quota timing. With Qingming holiday effects and weak stainless-steel demand, upstream supply accumulates, pushing ferronickel prices lower. | Flying Nickel announced completion of a community quarry partnership at its Minago nickel project in Manitoba. The project will strip near-surface limestone for 7,500 tonnes of construction material and build all-weather exploration roads to enable future drilling. The release frames this as engagement capacity building, not an output or supply change. | SMM reports nickel ore prices broadly unchanged WoW, with port stocks rising as the rainy season ends and Philippine transactions set to resume. However, high-grade NPI prices fell sharply WoW due to weak stainless steel demand, inventory accumulation, and lower stainless steel and nickel-related pricing, leading to a bearish outlook for April.

Talon Metals outlined its 2024 exploration plan for the Tamarack nickel-copper project in Minnesota and nickel targets in Michigan. The program includes a large drilling campaign and is partly funded by a US Department of Defense cost-share, boosting near-term expectations for expanded domestic nickel supply and sentiment. | Core Nickel Corp. announced hiring Integral Wealth Securities as a market maker for its shares on Canada’s CSE, a routine liquidity arrangement. The release also reiterates its nickel exploration properties and historical resource grades in Manitoba. Overall, it is more investor/stock related than a near-term nickel fundamentals driver. | Talon Metals provided an update on its 2024 drilling and exploration at the Tamarack nickel-copper-cobalt project in Minnesota and nickel prospects in Michigan. About half the program is funded by US DoD, with a 30,000-meter drilling plan aimed at expanding high-grade nickel-copper resources. The initiative could modestly support nickel sentiment. | SMM’s nickel sulfate daily review shows battery-grade nickel sulfate falling on April 3, with weaker transaction activity and lower downstream acceptance of high-priced material. The overall transaction price range has shifted down as spot tightness from early April gradually eases after increased supply, leaving continued downside risk. | SMM reports that on April 3 the main Shanghai nickel contract opened at 133,430 yuan/ton and closed at 133,450 yuan/ton, up 860 yuan/ton from the prior close. Trading was weaker with lower open interest, and the contract rebounded after an early dip before finishing +0.81% higher. | Couloir Capital initiated/updated research coverage of Fathom Nickel, highlighting continued 2024 exploration on two Saskatchewan properties. Initial drilling on the Albert Lake Project is underway with assays expected by late April, followed by additional surveys and more drilling on the Gochager Lake Property. The news is exploration-focused, with no clear market-supply impact. | Couloir Capital initiated and updated research coverage on Fathom Nickel, highlighting drilling at the Albert Lake and Gochager Lake properties in Saskatchewan. The firm expects assay results by end of April and further drilling later in 2024, potentially improving perceived nickel exploration prospects and near-term nickel sentiment. | Fathom Nickel announced it will present a live virtual corporate update webinar hosted by Red Cloud Financial Services on April 9, 2024. The release does not mention any project or production changes; it only invites registration and provides for a replay. Nickel market impact is likely limited.

Morgan Stanley’s commodity update highlights a strengthening bullish narrative for copper, expecting supply disruptions to drive a large deficit that persists into 2025, supported by AI/data-centre demand. It also prefers nickel for near-term upside based on rapid supply-side response to low prices and ongoing supply tightness. | The article examines how Indonesia can make its nickel downstreaming and mining more environmentally and economically sustainable. It highlights that export-ban disruption and oversupply have pressured nickel prices, and discusses potential measures like limiting smelter expansion, improving data and emissions controls, and using incentives more effectively. | London-traded base metals rose on April 4, led by copper hitting the highest level since January 2023. Gains were linked to a softer dollar, expectations of lower U.S. rates after Powell’s remarks, stronger-than-expected Chinese manufacturing data, and planned Chinese smelter output cuts amid raw-material shortages. | The article reviews Indonesia’s nickel downstreaming push—aiming for many new smelters—and ongoing controversies including oversupply-driven nickel price declines and the 2021 export-ban dispute. It suggests limiting expansion, reassessing incentives, and improving sustainability. Overall, the tone implies continued supply pressure and weak near-term nickel pricing. | Indonesian coal producer Harum Energy bought a 51% stake in Blue Sparking Energy, which operates nickel processing/refining and an HPAL project in North Maluku. Harum also previously increased its stake in nickel smelter Westron Metal. The moves reflect ongoing diversification into nickel production capacity. | Prospector Metals updated its Devon nickel project in Ontario, contracting a VTEM airborne survey and citing recent field sample results with up to 0.53% Ni and 1.125% Cu (plus PGE/Co trace). The company is also progressing other projects, but no production or concrete supply impacts are described. | Mosaic Minerals reported remaining drill results from its Gaboury project, confirming nickel potential over more than 4.5 km and identifying Ni-Cu magmatic mineralization potential. The geology also shows zinc, cobalt, copper and gold. The company plans further drilling and surveys, but no immediate production or supply-market change is provided. | Mosaic announced drilling results at its Gaboury Nickel project in Quebec, estimating intersection thickness at ~70% of the core interval and concluding the property has potential for magmatic Ni-Cu mineralization. Management plans more intensive drilling and surveys across magnetic anomalies, supporting expectations for additional nickel-bearing zones.

Japan, the US, and the Philippines plan to cooperate to strengthen the nickel supply chain, with a joint statement expected after the April 11 Washington summit. The Philippines is cited as a leading nickel producer, while China’s dominance in processing/refining is a key concern. EV and fuel-cell demand is expected to keep rising.

SHFE nickel rose for five straight days to around 133,450 yuan/mt by Apr 3. Slow Indonesian nickel ore approvals have left little new quota for two weeks, keeping ore premiums high and supply tight. Markets also watch potential effects from US/Japan/Philippines supply cooperation and macro expectations for June Fed cuts. | London copper slipped 0.6% as investors weighed uncertainty over fewer-than-expected U.S. Fed rate cuts, after strong jobs data reduced near-term cut expectations. In contrast, Shanghai copper rose 2.1% to a record high. LME aluminium and nickel fell, while SHFE aluminium and nickel increased. | Chalice Mining is seeking environmental approval to develop a mine based on Tim Goyder’s well-known nickel-copper discovery. If approved, it would reduce permitting uncertainty and improve expectations for future nickel and copper supply, which can provide a mild supportive effect on prices in the coming weeks. | SHFE nickel strengthened for five straight sessions, reaching 133,450 yuan/mt by April 3. The key driver is tighter Indonesian nickel ore supply as quota approvals are slower than expected, keeping ore premiums elevated. Market also weighs US/Japan/Philippines nickel cooperation after April 11 and Fed rate-cut timing. | Core Nickel announced it completed a 2024 winter drill program at its Halfway Lake nickel project in Manitoba, reporting visual sulfide mineralization in all three drillholes and expanded testing of the W62 Zone framework. Assay results are still pending, and no production, supply, or market structure changes were stated. | SMM reports China’s nickel pig iron output fell 2.82% m/m in March, while ferronickel output declined due to slower Indonesia RKAB approvals, rainy-season disruptions in the Philippines, tighter nickel ore supply, and ongoing profit inversion leading some plants to stop. April output is expected to keep declining, supporting nickel demand/supply balance. | Australian miner Andrew Forrest urges China and other buyers to demand higher environmental standards for nickel supply from Indonesia, after Indonesian processing faced criticism. The push aligns with LME changes to report low-carbon nickel trading and follows prior nickel price declines tied to heavy Indonesian supply. | Nickel prices reversed after peaking around March 13, falling about 5.56% month over month. Stainless market sentiment softened as nickel retraced and distributors described Q1 as slow. LME nickel inventories ballooned to the highest since May 2022, reinforcing a bearish near-term outlook amid still-oversupplied conditions.

The article reports copper futures rising to a 14-month high above $4.24/lb, up 5.67% for the week. It links the rally to strong demand for solar panels and added supply risk from disruptions at major mines, citing logistical problems in Congo and droughts in Zambia. ETFs also gained alongside copper. | Nickel Industries is seeing strong investor interest for its Indonesian HPAL project, aiming to supply 70,000–80,000 tpa of nickel products from Sulawesi in 2H25, with some stake potentially sold from Tsingshan. With Indonesian supply rising and oversupply already depressing LME nickel, added output prospects are bearish. | Nickel Industries says over 25 car and battery makers are interested in buying nickel from its Indonesia HPAL smelter, aimed at producing nickel cathode and battery feedstocks. The firm argues it may qualify for LME low-carbon nickel, but buyers want lower prices. Recent oversupply pressured nickel sharply in 2024. | PTX Metals provided an update on its W2 Cu-Ni-PGE project drill program, including progress on a planned 2,500-metre campaign and ongoing modelling of historical drill data after recent claim consolidation. It cites historical grades averaging 0.58% copper and 0.37% nickel but provides no production impact. | Talon Metals outlined progress on Minnesota permitting for its Tamarack nickel-copper-cobalt project and development of a DOE-funded Battery Minerals Processing Facility in North Dakota. The update includes NEPA/trial site steps and R&D partnerships aimed at improving nickel recovery and increasing copper recovery from concentrates, supporting future U.S. supply. | Talon Metals outlined progress on permitting for its Tamarack nickel-copper project and work toward its DOE/grant-funded North Dakota battery mineral processing facility. The company highlights accelerated exploration and processing plans to improve nickel recovery and concentrate quality, alongside R&D that could improve copper recoveries. The overall outlook is supportive for nickel prices. | Talon Metals provides an update on permitting for its Tamarack underground nickel-copper project and progress toward a DOE-funded North Dakota battery mineral processing facility. The company highlights steps to secure an industrial site, conduct NEPA/tribal consultation, and advance processing to increase nickel recovery. Overall, it supports expectations for domestic nickel supply. | The article highlights an upcoming US CPI release that will shape Federal Reserve expectations for interest-rate cuts. Core inflation is expected to ease slightly to 3.7% year-on-year. Falling Treasury yields and steadier US shares support risk sentiment, which is generally bullish for industrial metals like copper and, indirectly, aluminium and nickel. | Violent unrest in New Caledonia is disrupting access to nickel mining and transport routes near extraction sites, with trucks blocked and roads intermittently closed. Northern authorities threaten to suspend mining permits over unpaid guarantees, while the French “nickel pact” seeks industry reforms amid political deadlock, raising near-term nickel supply risk.

China’s refined nickel output was 24,900 mt in March, slightly higher MoM and up strongly YoY, but down versus January due to maintenance shutdowns. SMM forecasts April refined nickel production around 25,900 mt, about +4% MoM, as suspended plants restart and new capacity ramps up. | China’s nickel sulphate output rose sharply in March on resumed wet-process recycling and demand recovery, keeping supply comparatively tight. With NCM chain destocking easing and EV auto sales improving, smelters raised operating rates. April output is expected to increase but still face demand support, sustaining higher nickel sulphate prices. | Aluminium prices surged to a 14-month peak as hedge funds bought the sector on optimism for the global economy. Better-than-expected manufacturing expansion in China and the U.S. boosted demand expectations. LME copper neared a 14-month high, while nickel also gained, reflecting broad bullish fund flows. | SMM reports the average price of SMM8–12% high-nickel pig iron rose slightly to 931.5 yuan per nickel point. High nickel ore costs kept Indonesian ferronickel output relatively steady, while some China producers paused. Post-holiday sentiment improved with higher stainless steel disk prices. | Fastmarkets reports sharp month-on-month swings in Shanghai bonded stocks: aluminium jumped over 150% as sellers held back expecting better future arbitrage terms; nickel bonded inventories fell due to exports; copper bonded inventories rose as prolonged arbitrage losses reduced imports and reflected smelter routings/maintenance-driven delivery changes. | Nickel premiums in Europe (briquettes, uncut cathode and 4×4) were unchanged week-on-week, supported by some Indonesian material inflows but with quiet trading. In China, nickel full plate premiums were also steady as import arbitrage losses widened and overseas demand remained sluggish, keeping prices range-bound.

Indonesia has surged as the world’s leading nickel refiner, supported by cheap coal, ore and labour, and claims it has pushed prices so low that peers face losses. It also notes potential exits by companies like BHP and Glencore, implying ongoing supply pressure for nickel despite investment tied to batteries and EVs. | The report describes Indonesia’s rise as the world’s top nickel refiner, powered by coal and supported by Chinese investment, alongside severe local pollution and alleged environmental violations. It cites heavy-metal contamination, worker safety concerns after a deadly smelter explosion, and potential scrutiny—factors that can raise nickel risk and weigh on prices. | Copper prices traded in a tight range as the U.S. dollar firmed after stronger-than-expected U.S. inflation, pushing back expectations for rate cuts and weighing on metal demand. Support came from supply-side disruptions and improving China manufacturing. Aluminium edged up slightly; nickel and tin fell on the session. | Canada Nickel says it has begun FEED for the Crawford nickel sulphide project, with engineering led by Ausenco. Management expects FEED completion before a mid-2025 construction decision and first production by year-end 2027. The update supports improving nickel project development outlook and potential supply expectations. | Flying Nickel announced an updated Mineral Resource Estimate for its Minago nickel-PGM project, reporting a 41.95% increase in in-pit measured & indicated nickel and an inaugural platinum and palladium resource. The estimate uses nickel/Pt/Pd price assumptions for pit optimization but gives no near-term production or inventory changes. | Canada Nickel says it has started front-end engineering design for its Crawford nickel sulphide project, led by Ausenco, while continuing financing and permitting. Management targets a mid-2025 construction decision and first production by year-end 2027, implying longer-dated nickel supply potential but no immediate output change.

The article forecasts nickel to remain rangebound in 2024 despite a holdover surplus from 2023. Indonesia’s expanding output and China’s production keep supply elevated, while China’s slower real-estate recovery weighs on demand. INSG expects oversupply to persist; EV and stainless demand offer partial support. | The U.S., Japan and Philippines outlined “Luzon Economic Corridor” initiatives, including U.S. funding plans for a Philippine ore-to-nickel and cobalt processing plant by Eramen Minerals. The cooperation also covers infrastructure and logistics, which could support nickel project development, though output timing is not immediate. | Refined nickel inventories increased in April: social stocks rose 16% MoM and bonded zone inventory gained 12% MoM. SMM links the rise to stronger exports from domestic plate producers as SHFE–LME price spreads widened, tepid downstream buying, and arrivals of overseas plates late March. | China’s refined nickel customs clearance/import volumes dropped sharply in January-February, indicating continued weakening domestic demand. The article attributes the fall to sluggish downstream demand and increased substitution toward newer electro-deposited nickel brands, which are cheaper and comparable to Russian material. Further declines are expected in March. | SMM reports Shanghai nickel’s main contract opened at 138,820 yuan/ton and closed at 138,250 yuan/ton, down 1.19% on the day. Intraday trading weakened, with lower open interest and reduced long/short activity. Price rose after a soft start, peaking near 138,990, then eased into the close. | Canada Nickel says it has started FEED for the Crawford nickel project in Ontario and is advancing financing and permitting toward a mid-2025 construction decision and first production by end-2027. The large stated nickel resource and future nickel-cobalt output support incremental bullish sentiment for nickel prices. | The article title says the US is taking action targeting Russian aluminum, copper and nickel. US restrictions on Russian metal exports or trade flows would likely affect near-term supply expectations, with price moves reflecting tighter availability and changes in market sourcing for all three metals. | The article’s headline says the US is taking action targeting Russian aluminium, copper and nickel. Such measures against Russian producers or trade flows can reduce near-term availability and increase supply risk, which typically shifts expectations toward tighter sourcing conditions over the coming days and weeks. | The US and UK prohibited trading exchanges from accepting new Russian aluminum, copper and nickel and barred imports into the US and UK. The move targets disruption of Russian export revenue. Existing exchange stocks are exempt, but LME data show Russia dominates warehouse inventories, supporting tighter supply expectations. | The US Treasury announced a ban on importing Russian-origin aluminium, copper, and nickel, with limited exceptions for pre-April-13-2024 material. The sanctions also restrict use of Russian metals on global exchanges and OTC derivatives, preventing LME and CME acceptance of Russian supplies, boosting expectations of tighter market availability. | The US Treasury announced a ban on importing Russian-origin aluminium, copper, and nickel, with exceptions for pre-April-13-2024 production or licensing. The measures also restrict use of Russian metals on global exchanges and derivative markets, including prohibiting LME and CME from accepting Russian metal. | The US and UK prohibited metal-trading exchanges from accepting new Russian aluminum, copper, and nickel and barred imports into the US and UK, aiming to disrupt Russian export revenue. Exchanges exempt existing Russian stocks, but the ban removes incremental supply, tightening availability. | The US Treasury announced a ban on importing Russian-origin aluminum, copper, and nickel, with limited exceptions, from April 13 onward. The measure also restricts Russian metals’ use on global exchanges and OTC derivatives, including LME and CME acceptance, likely tightening supply and supporting prices. | The US and UK prohibited metal trading exchanges from accepting new Russian aluminum, copper and nickel and barred imports into the US and UK. The measures target Russian export revenue while exempting existing stocks on exchanges to limit market stability. Russia is a major producer, and Russian-origin shares are high in LME inventories.

Poseidon Nickel says Mineral Resources is trying to back out of terms for the sale of a nickel concentrator and tenements at Lake Johnston, WA. Ardea Resources says it has secured $98m from major Japanese companies to help fund its very large Aussie laterite nickel project. The company also highlighted a strong borehole electromagnetic anomaly and thick (~up to 10m) mineralized accumulations, implying continued upside for nickel and copper prospectivity. | Talon Metals reported 14 new drill holes intersecting nickel-copper mineralization in Minnesota’s Raptor Zone, plus a strong borehole electromagnetic anomaly suggesting continuity and potential thicker sulphide zones. Assays are pending and no production or inventory changes are cited, limiting immediate effects on copper and nickel prices. | Talon Metals announced 14 new drill holes intersecting nickel-copper mineralization in the Raptor Zone at its Tamarack project in Minnesota, along with a strong borehole electromagnetic anomaly. Assays are pending and no production changes are stated, limiting immediate near-term impacts to nickel and copper prices. | Talon Metals announced 14 new drill holes intersecting nickel-copper mineralization in the Raptor Zone at its Tamarack project in Minnesota, with assays pending. The company also highlighted a strong borehole electromagnetic anomaly and thick mineralized intervals, supporting exploration prospects but without immediate production impact. | Zimbabwe placed its only operating nickel mine, the Trojan Nickel Mine, under administration after production was halted for equipment failure and low market prices. Output has already collapsed and restart plans were delayed due to low prices and high input costs. Administration could further delay supply, supporting nickel prices. | Zimbabwe placed its only operating nickel mine, the Trojan mine, under administration after it halted production months earlier due to equipment failure and low prices. The mine’s output has fallen sharply, with restart delayed over low prices and high electricity/input costs, reducing expected near-term nickel supply.

The overnight report says base metal prices are falling again as renewed Chinese stimulus fails to support prices, with China closed until Tuesday. It notes the US dollar is down with lower yields and the Australian dollar higher, but the overall base-metals tone remains bearish. | Copper prices bounced on Friday as the U.S. dollar weakened and forecast revisions pointed to diminished supply after mine disruptions.
