That supply-effect potential can shift sentiment in nickel pricing over the coming days to weeks. | New Caledonia’s Koniambo (KNS) nickel plant began mass layoff processes after being mothballed, with a deadline for a “cold” sleep state as ownership and buyer options remain unresolved. The country’s unrest has also targeted nickel sites, adding supply disruption amid a broader nickel-price crisis. | Canada Nickel reported improved testwork at its Crawford East Zone, including a locked-cycle result delivering 47% total nickel recovery and 60% nickel sulphide concentrate, with open-circuit tests averaging 28% higher recovery than modelled. A 130-tonne pilot plant began commissioning and will run until September to generate concentrate samples. | First Atlantic Nickel reports progress at its Atlantic Nickel Project in Newfoundland, including a completed investor site visit, a bridge installed for year-round access, permits filed for additional road work, and expanded geological activity targeting awaruite nickel occurrences. The updates support ongoing exploration that may bolster longer-term nickel supply expectations. | Reuters reports copper fell on Monday due to concerns over weak Chinese demand and elevated inventories. LME three-month copper dropped about 0.7% to $9,045/ton, with LME stocks more than doubling since early June. Macro expectations around Fed rate cuts also influenced sentiment across metals. | Nornickel reported a 1% nickel output drop in H1 due to furnace repairs at the Nadezhda smelter but kept full-year nickel guidance at 184,000–194,000 tonnes. It also noted lingering constraints from delayed Western-sourced equipment and the US/UK nickel/aluminium/copper trading and import bans, with repairs resuming early August. | Fastmarkets reports China nickel sulfate prices edging down, citing slow demand, weaker liquidity, and continued declines in the three-month LME nickel cash price. It also links softer international nickel sulfate activity to BHP’s suspension at Nickel West and West Musgrave, weighing on market sentiment. | Vedanta Nico, a Vedanta subsidiary, partnered with US battery technology firm AEsir Technologies to supply nickel for next-generation nickel-zinc batteries for EVs, 5G telecom and critical infrastructure. The move highlights growing nickel demand for battery cathodes, potentially supporting near-term nickel price sentiment.

Tanzania agreed to pay US$90 million in instalments to Australia’s Indiana Resources to settle an ICSID dispute over cancellation of its Ntaka Hill nickel licence. The article describes the project’s nickel sulphide potential but offers no concrete near-term production impact, implying minimal effect on global nickel pricing. | Kenorland Minerals entered a definitive agreement to sell 100% of its South Thompson nickel project to Evolution Nickel, receiving Evolution shares and a 2% net smelter return royalty. Closing is expected before end-2024 and depends on Evolution raising at least C$4m. The release is about exploration development, not production. | FPX Nickel reports value engineering at its Baptiste project reduced estimated carbon intensity by 50% (to about 1.2 tCO2e/tNi), driven by trolley-assist haul trucks cutting diesel use ~50%. It also secured BC Hydro funding for electrification studies, improving economics and expected nickel operating competitiveness.

PTX Metals reported additional near-surface drill results from its W2 Cu-Ni-PGE project in Ontario, citing higher confidence in continuity of mineralized zones and new intersections with disseminated to massive sulphides. The update is exploration-focused and does not signal near-term production impacts. Gold assays are also pending next week. | PTX Metals released additional diamond-drill assay results from its 100%-owned W2 Cu-Ni-PGE project, expanding and confirming near-surface sulphide mineralization across several zones. Results increase confidence in continuity of historical zones. The release is exploratory and does not mention production, shipments, or new supply disruptions affecting prices. | Indonesia expanded its Simbara mining tracking system to cover nickel (and tin), following a corruption case and to improve governance. The move links production, sales, exports, royalties, and port clearance, targeting Rp 5–10 trillion in additional nickel-related royalties/PNBP. It should tighten oversight and reduce illegal supply. | Merdeka Copper Gold (MDKA) is shifting its mining energy use from coal to renewables such as solar, alongside B35 biodiesel for vehicles and feasibility work for hydropower and wind. The transition is tied to operations including the Wetar copper mine and MDKA’s nickel assets, framed as cost-efficient ESG that may support output continuity. | Indonesia’s Nickel Industries managing director said Indonesia may limit further Chinese investment in its nickel sector to avoid reliance on China and attract more Western capital. This policy risk can influence expectations for Indonesia’s nickel supply and near-term project development, offering support to nickel prices over the next weeks.

Copper prices fell at the start of August as concerns grew over weakening Chinese demand, supported by a private survey showing manufacturing contracted and an official PMI hitting a five-month low. Despite some support from a narrower export parity and retreating inventories, higher exports boosted inventories. Powell’s rate-cut hint offered limited offset.

Indonesia’s Geological Agency says it has identified more than 100 additional potential nickel reserve sites for further exploration. The government’s findings reinforce Indonesia’s role as the leading nickel producer, important for EV batteries. With output and export revenues already large, new prospective supply may ease supply concerns. | Indonesia’s Geological Agency says it has identified over 100 additional potential nickel reserve sites for further exploration. The findings reinforce Indonesia’s role as the leading nickel producer used in EV batteries, but the report does not point to near-term production increases or export changes, suggesting limited immediate price pressure. | London copper rose 0.4% but was set for a flat week as investors await US jobs data while China’s demand outlook remains downbeat. Copper inventories stay high. LME aluminium edged down. Nickel prices were supported by tight Indonesian ore supply, but overall the week’s direction appears muted. | Indonesia’s Geological Agency says it has identified more than 100 new potential nickel reserve locations and will continue exploration. The update strengthens Indonesia’s position as the leading global nickel producer used in EV batteries. With large existing output already, new prospects may support expectations for longer-term supply, mildly bullish for nickel. | Goldrea Resources announced LOIs to acquire 50% interests in additional mineral claims adjoining its Cannonball Project, which hosts multiple porphyry copper-gold and copper-gold prospects. The company says the geology matches Seabridge’s Golden Triangle corridor and highlights recommended drilling. No production volumes, inventories, or near-term supply changes are reported.

London copper edged up 0.2% after prior sell-offs, as some dip-buying emerged, but global demand concerns capped upside. The U.S. jobs report and weaker China activity fueled recession worries while expectations of Fed cuts supported industrial demand. LME aluminium and nickel rose slightly, showing mixed sentiment. | Copper prices tumbled as worsening China and U.S. demand outlooks sparked a sell-off. Weaker U.S. jobs data and technology earnings reinforced recession fears, while rising LME warehouse inventories weighed near term. The risk-off move also pushed aluminium and nickel modestly lower alongside copper. | Indonesia’s Geological Agency reported at least 100 additional, currently unexplored sites with potential nickel reserves. It said the findings strengthen Indonesia’s position as the leading global nickel producer, with nickel being used in EV batteries, and that further exploration will continue. | Copper fell sharply (up to 3.8% on the LME) as global financial contagion triggered a risk-off rush to cash out and turn bearish amid fears of a US recession. The selloff pressured other industrial raw-materials, reflecting weaker growth expectations and tighter financial conditions.

Eramet Indonesia’s director says the company, via PT Eramet Bumi Sulawesi, is looking to expand its role in Indonesia’s nickel sector and build partnerships for an end-to-end EV battery value chain. Eramet plans new mining-site work and permits, positioning against Chinese dominance amid Western EV supply concerns. | Asian Battery Metals reported interim reconnaissance results from Mongolia’s Yambat project, including evidence of an expanded magmatic copper-nickel system and new targets such as Copper Ridge. Mapping and geophysics guided sampling and a Phase 1 drilling campaign. Results are preliminary and plan further scout drilling, implying future supply potential. | Indonesia’s nickel hydrometallurgy intermediate product output fell 12.06% MoM in June due to abnormal weather, which reduced transportation efficiency of ore and materials and caused delayed raw material supply. August output is forecast to rise 9.73% MoM as weather improves, though levels remain below normal.

London copper fell 0.5% to a more than four-and-a-half month low as a firm dollar and looming U.S. recession fears pressured prices, alongside weak China demand concerns. LME aluminium also slipped 0.3% and nickel fell 0.5%, while Shanghai contracts were mixed, reflecting catch-up activity. | Reuters reports LME three-month copper fell 0.5% to a four-and-a-half-month low due to a firm dollar and worries about U.S. and Chinese growth. Shanghai copper rose 0.3% as higher prices spurred Chinese exports and narrowing spreads attracted cargoes. LME aluminium and nickel slipped slightly while SHFE aluminium rose and nickel edged down. | ESG-focused coverage includes Macquarie’s notes that several nickel mines are being suspended/closed amid weak pricing, which may tighten near-term supply. It also reports South32 is appealing WA EPA conditions at its Worsley alumina mine, potentially influencing aluminium supply/costs. Other sections are largely macro/energy/ESG themes. | The piece argues Indonesia’s nickel boom—driven by EV and battery demand—has caused major rainforest and coastal damage, citing relentless smelting smoke, mining craters, and pollution at processing hubs like IMIP. It notes Indonesia’s leading nickel output and smelting capacity, raising the risk of disruption or tighter oversight affecting near-term nickel supply.

The article reviews Minnesota Governor Tim Walz’s approach to copper-nickel mining and highlights that Twin Metals’ leases were pulled by the Biden Administration, effectively halting a planned mine near the BWCA. Walz is portrayed as hands-off and emphasizing federal environmental review, keeping project timing uncertain. | Copper edged up slightly on a softer U.S. dollar, but broader sentiment is weak as LME inventories jumped and global growth expectations deteriorated. Commentary notes rising TC/RCs suggest improving concentrate supply. Aluminium and nickel prices were also lower in the same session, indicating mixed but generally pressured industrial metals. | Copper prices steadied but remained under pressure as pessimism on global growth and risk-off sentiment weighed on base metals. LME copper inventories surged nearly threefold to 294,750 tons. Aluminium and nickel also fell on the day, reflecting weaker demand expectations outweighing improving spot refining charges. | Golden Arrow reported new step-out drill intercepts at its San Pietro IOCG project in Chile, including long intervals grading about 0.43–0.44% Cu with associated gold and cobalt. The release is an exploration update with no production or reserve change stated, so any near-term effect on copper prices is likely limited.

Copper rose Friday after U.S. jobless claims came in better than expected, easing recession-demand fears and supporting rate-cut optimism. It still faces a fifth straight weekly drop, with LME inventories at a high since 2019 limiting upside catalysts. Aluminium and nickel also climbed amid the broader rebound. | Ormonde notes Peak Nickel has resumed a 2024 drill programme at its Rodburn Ni-Cu-Co project in Scotland, targeting further definition and potential expansion of mineralization. The announcement includes Ni, Cu and Co grades used in Ni-equivalent assumptions, but provides no new price-driving results or production impacts. | SMM reports high-grade NPI (8–12% nickel) prices rose WoW on tight nickel ore availability and higher ore-driven smelter costs, limiting short-term supply increases. Easing rainfall in Weda Bay supports logistics, while stainless-steel peak-season demand lifts inquiries. Nickel prices consolidated with a rebound and upside bias, though fundamentals remain weak.

Copper prices opened the week softer as investors weighed ongoing macroeconomic uncertainty and awaited data for demand signals. Reuters cites economic worries including recession fears, with copper down on both LME and Shanghai. Nickel also slipped, while aluminium was steadier, reflecting cautious industrial demand sentiment. | Copper prices rose as traders covered shorts and looked to upcoming China and U.S. data for demand and rate-cut timing. LME copper was up about 0.9%, with cancelled warrants increasing and warehouse inventories still elevated. The report also said aluminium and nickel climbed on the same risk-on backdrop. | US and Indonesia discussed a possible Critical Minerals Specific Free Trade Agreement to increase access to Indonesian nickel for EV batteries. However, nine US senators sent a bipartisan letter urging scrutiny over labor, environmental issues, and Chinese dominance, potentially slowing or complicating the agreement and dampening near-term nickel sentiment. | LME data show Russian-origin aluminium’s share in LME-approved warehouses rose to 65% in July (from 50% in June), while Indian-origin fell. Total on-warrant aluminium stocks fell 23% in July. Russian copper and nickel shares declined. The piece also references the LME’s earlier ban on sanctioned Russian output. | LME data show Russian-origin aluminium’s share of on-warrant stocks rose to 65% in July while Indian-origin fell to 33%, alongside a 23% decline in total on-warrant aluminium stocks. The May Russian ban remains cited, with similar share changes for copper and nickel inventories. | Knights Bay Oryx’s Oman subsidiary has completed initial drilling at its nickel laterite project in Mineral Block 21, targeting nickel continuity and staged resource definition. The company has not yet released lab results, but sampled ore near small mines showed ~1% nickel and shipped material for metallurgical testing. | Nickel prices continued to plunge through July, with early August stabilizing near early-2024 levels. Stainless steel distributors report weak demand, and lower nickel-driven stainless surcharges reduce buyer incentives. U.S. manufacturing PMI stays in contraction, reinforcing soft end-use conditions. Outokumpu sees growth QoQ but still down YoY.

Terrain Minerals reports airborne geophysics at its Lort River project in Western Australia, identifying five conductors that may indicate “Nova-style” magmatic nickel-copper sulphide mineralisation similar to IGO’s Nova-Bollinger deposits. The company plans ground EM, soil geochemistry, and reconnaissance drilling, seeking a new nickel discovery. | Morgan Stanley flags nickel may be near its pricing floor: inventories are surging and demand remains challenging, limiting upside and keeping prices range-bound. For copper, spot has retraced and Macquarie cuts short- to medium-term demand and price forecasts, pointing to renewed near-term downside pressure despite M&A activity. | Morgan Stanley says nickel’s “bottom” may be near but elevated nickel inventories and challenging demand constrain sustained rallies, implying range-bound pricing. Macquarie expects copper volatility, cuts 2025–27 forecasts on weaker demand, and lowers 2024 Chinese and North American growth estimates. Overall, neither metal faces a clear immediate breakout. | Reuters reports copper prices fell as LME inventories climbed to near five-year highs and China demand remained sluggish. Traders cited exports of surplus copper into Asian LME warehouses and weaker Chinese macro data. Purchases from some physical consumers and hopes of a BHP Escondida strike limited losses, but overall sentiment stayed bearish. | Grid Metals provided a permitting and exploration update for its Manitoba projects. Donner’s advanced exploration permit for lithium was approved but bulk sampling is deferred due to lower lithium prices. For MM, the company plans to resume a ~5,000 m drill program targeting Cu-rich sulfide mineralization near the New Manitoba deposit.

Australia’s FIRB approved Japanese investors (Sumitomo Metal Mining and Mitsubishi) to participate in Ardea Resources’ Goongarrie Hub within the Kalgoorlie Nickel Project. The decision removes a JV condition, enabling continued feasibility-study funding and engineering work, supporting bullish sentiment for nickel development. | NewRange Copper Nickel said it will run four studies to upgrade tailings storage design, modernize water treatment (with $100 million planned), improve efficient production to potentially raise throughput, and explore electric ore transport. The copper-nickel project uses existing processing infrastructure and faces permitting work, but most permits remain active. | The report says developers of a stalled Minnesota copper-nickel mine plan studies that could result in significant changes. Such actions can influence market expectations for near-term copper and nickel supply. However, the provided text lacks details on whether production will resume, delay, expand, or be canceled, limiting direction.

China’s July primary aluminium output rose 6% to 3.68m tonnes, the highest monthly total in over 20 years, as smelters ramped up due to still-profitable operations. While prices recently fell and demand stayed weak, analysts expect profits to remain steady and output to rise further, weighing on aluminium sentiment. | NewRange Copper Nickel (PolyMet) developers say they will run studies over the next year on redesign options to improve environmental safeguards, reduce carbon emissions, optimize waste storage and water treatment, and change ore transport. Environmental groups argue the plan is flawed. No decisions or production timing changes are announced. | NewRange Copper Nickel (PolyMet) developers said they will run studies on environmental safeguards and cost/energy efficiency, potentially changing the open-pit mine and processing design. Any major changes would require additional reviews and permitting. The project’s further delays/uncertainty are likely a modest negative for near-term copper and nickel sentiment. | Nickel prices have fallen sharply, reflecting a supply glut in Indonesia and, increasingly, weaker EV battery demand. Europe and the US are seeing EV incentive cutbacks and slower downstream uptake of nickel-rich chemistries (NCM/NCA). Producers are impairing and delaying battery materials investments, reinforcing bearish sentiment for nickel.

Copper prices are set for their first weekly gain in six, supported by mine supply worries after a strike at BHP’s Escondida in Chile and stronger-than-feared U.S. retail sales and jobs data easing recession fears. Aluminium and nickel also ticked higher, reflecting improved base-metals sentiment. | Philippine laterite nickel ore prices increased over the past week, with medium-grade ore rising from about $45/wmt to ~$48/wmt. Demand from Indonesia, tight ore supply, and smelter restocking needs are cited, while policy rewards and expectations of further quota tightness could keep prices rising short term. | Flying Nickel Mining completed its merger, acquiring 100% of Nevada Vanadium, creating a wholly owned subsidiary. The company is primarily focused on advancing its Minago nickel project and a vanadium project. The corporate combination may affect longer-term sentiment, but no near-term production or supply changes are stated.

Copper prices rose as U.S. recession fears eased after strong U.S. data and China’s copper inventories fell. LME three-month copper climbed 0.9% and SHFE September gained 0.5%. The report also cites easing supply concerns at BHP’s Escondida after a wage deal, alongside gains in aluminium and a modest nickel rise. | Flying Nickel Mining has finalized the acquisition of Nevada Vanadium, making it a wholly owned subsidiary. The company is an exploration-stage miner focused on nickel sulphide and vanadium, including development of its Minago nickel project in Manitoba. The article details share issuance and governance changes, with no stated output timing changes. | Power Nickel reported final results from a winter drilling program at its Nisk project’s “Lion Zone,” saying the mineralized zone increased over 94% and providing assay intercepts. The company is pursuing further summer drilling to test depth and continuity, aiming to expand high-grade nickel-copper mineralization.

London LME three-month copper fell 0.4% as the previous rally was attributed to short-covering rather than new demand. Open interest declined and analysts noted limited contract rollover. Chinese physical demand improved slightly, and an Escondida strike was averted, easing supply fears. LME aluminium and nickel also declined. | SMM cited China Customs data showing China’s nickel ore imports fell to 3.4851 million wmt in July (-30.6% YoY), with laterite down sharply. Philippines shipments dipped due to weather, though constraints are easing and supply is shifting toward Indonesia. August is expected to see room for laterite growth despite domestic demand. | Falcon Gold reported delays from high water levels at its Great Burnt Copper Project in Newfoundland and the need for additional permitting to build 8 km of road. Equipment mobilization is pending completion by week-end, after which drilling will test multiple copper geophysical anomalies. No nickel operational changes were reported. | Falcon Gold reports delays and permitting for drill access at its Great Burnt Copper Project in Newfoundland, with roadwork expected to finish by week-end. The property is contiguous to Benton/Homeland Nickel’s Great Burnt Copper Zone. Reported past and recent drilling shows high copper grades, but no immediate production impact.

Zeb Nickel Corp. said South Africa’s DMPR granted an Integrated Environmental Authorisation for its Limpopo Zeb Nickel Project, clearing a major permitting phase after a lengthy EIA process. The approval supports progressing toward a mining right for nickel extraction, with additional associated copper mineralisation mentioned. | Reports say BHP is discussing potential investment with Indonesia’s government to expand/participate in Indonesia’s rapidly growing nickel processing industry. The backdrop is BHP suspending WA nickel operations due to global nickel oversupply from Indonesia’s export ban. Such investment talks may support longer-term nickel supply balance expectations near term. | Eco-Business says Indonesia’s nickel push faces major hurdles: low-grade product focus, ore supply gaps, heavy captive-coal emissions, and environmental/labor controversies. EU and US rules may reduce appeal of Indonesian processed nickel, and a $2.6 billion BASF–Eramet project was cancelled. The article warns of China dependence and possible nickel price weakness. | Zeb Nickel received integrated environmental authorisation from South Africa’s Department of Minerals and Petroleum Resources for its Bushveld complex project. The approval supports progress toward a mining right and allows activities under the approved EIA, with plans for further drilling to improve nickel grade and project economics. | Flying Nickel Mining entered a definitive arrangement to sell its Minago nickel assets in Manitoba to Norway House Cree Nation for C$8 million cash plus shares. The transaction is expected by mid-October 2024 and requires shareholder and regulatory approvals. The announcement is asset-specific and not linked to broader nickel supply or pricing changes.

Flying Nickel Mining signed a definitive deal to sell its Minago nickel project in Manitoba to Norway House Cree Nation, receiving $8m cash and a 11.4% share stake transfer. The company plans a rebrand and remains active in its Nevada vanadium interests. The transaction is unlikely to meaningfully shift near-term global nickel balances. | NewRange Copper Nickel advances permitting-critical studies for a potential NorthMet copper-nickel mine near Hoyt Lakes, including tailings, water treatment, and efficiency upgrades. The company is examining higher throughput (32,000 to 40,000 tons/day) to increase domestic copper and nickel supply, which could lift near-term price sentiment.

SMM reports the coefficient for high-grade nickel matte versus LME nickel rose to 87–88 as nickel prices fell and refining costs increased. It also cites weak downstream demand, substitution pressure from MHP due to high coefficients, and producers shifting toward NPI for better margins, leaving transactions sluggish. | Nornickel reported a 22% profit drop, blaming weak nickel prices, logistics disruptions in the Red Sea, and harder cross-border payments. Maritime restrictions caused nickel and copper stock accumulation and reduced sales. The firm cited a 28% LME nickel price fall in H1 and forecast a 2024/25 nickel surplus.

First Atlantic Nickel reports completion of Phase 1 infrastructure upgrades and ongoing sampling over a 30 km awaruite (nickel-iron alloy) trend in Newfoundland, ahead of a planned 2024 drilling program. The release highlights awaruite’s easier concentration versus sulfide nickel and potential to ease North American nickel concentrate shortages. | Lifezone Metals said it is starting an evaluation with the U.S. International Development Finance Corporation for political risk insurance for future investments in the Kabanga nickel-copper-cobalt mine and Kahama hydromet refinery in Tanzania. The project is expected to produce LME-grade nickel and copper, with a feasibility study progressing. | DRC authorities have halted or recommended stopping Chemaf Resources’s planned sale of its copper-cobalt mines to China’s Norinco, citing violations of Gecamines’ lease and farm-out contract terms. The dispute may disrupt near-term copper supply expectations from the DRC, though the final outcome is uncertain. | First Atlantic Nickel reported Phase 1 infrastructure upgrades at its Atlantic Nickel Project in Newfoundland, including road/bridge improvements and expanded nickel sampling along a 30 km trend. The firm is also using LiDAR for better geological targeting and sending initial samples for analysis, aimed at developing awaruite nickel-iron resources. | South Pacific Metals reports exceptional surface trench assay results at its Osena gold-copper Osena project in Papua New Guinea, including 79 m averaging ~981 ppm Cu and localized higher copper grades. The findings support potential expansion of a porphyry system, but are early-stage exploration with limited near-term impact on copper prices and none on nickel or aluminium. | Fastmarkets reports China nickel sulfate prices are rangebound but generally supported. Tight nickel MHP supply (and firm MHP payable terms) and low salt production inventories help hold prices firm, while slower NCM cathode precursor demand limits gains. Higher LME nickel cash prices lifted the all-in nickel sulfate range. | Nornickel said it plans to transfer part of its copper-plant capacities from Norilsk to China via a joint venture, aiming to resolve market-access and sanctions-related issues. Experts warn this could disrupt Russia’s industrial base and concentrate flows. The relocation can affect near-term expectations for copper supply and related nickel-linked outputs.

Anglesey Mining says it has submitted an initial Environmental Impact Assessment Scoping Report to restart underground mining at Parys Mountain, historically a major copper producer. The plan would process concentrate (~30% copper) for export, but requires significant investment and further approvals, making near-term copper impact uncertain. | The UK nuclear regulator (ONR) issued improvement notices to Sellafield after breaches of hazardous chemical controls involving nickel nitrate in an effluent facility. Contamination outside a glovebox could have exposed workers. The issues must be corrected by late 2024 and March 2025, without compromising nuclear safety.

Rusal reported a 41.6% jump in first-half profit as it increased production of primary aluminium and alumina and boosted shipments to China to record levels. It also notes the LME broad ban on Russian aluminium, copper and nickel amid U.S./UK sanctions. Improved volumes and rerouting are mildly supportive for prices. | Rusal’s first-half adjusted net profit rose 41.6% as it increased production and shipped record volumes to China. The firm faced demand pressure as Western buyers avoid Russia-made metal after Ukraine. The article notes April LME bans on Russian aluminium, copper and nickel and links improved output to higher prices for aluminium and alumina. | Argentina’s minister says the pro-business mining incentive regime (RIGI) should attract major foreign investment and help new lithium and copper projects start later this year. Copper is framed as crucial for EVs and AI, with large capex needs for projects, implying improved near-term supply/export expectations.  | Sumitomo’s Ambatovy nickel mine in Madagascar missed output targets after malfunctions, prompting an 89 billion yen write-down and a restructuring plan with a British court. Annual output was 31,000 tonnes vs 40,000 targeted. The company expects costs and production to improve once operations stabilize.

Ardea Resources executed a binding shareholders’ agreement with a Japanese consortium (Sumitomo Metal Mining and Mitsubishi) to fund a $98.5m definitive feasibility study and advance FEED for the Goongarrie Hub nickel-cobalt project in Western Australia. The step supports potential FID but does not change near-term nickel output.

KNS, a New Caledonian nickel producer involving Glencore, shut its furnaces over the weekend, moving into care and maintenance and laying off most of its 1,200 staff. The wider local nickel sector has been hit by unrest, with Eramet’s SLN operating minimally and Prony Resources suspending operations.

Canada Nickel filed an NI 43-101 technical report for its Deloro nickel sulphide project, confirming an initial mineral resource estimate effective July 17, 2024 with no material differences from earlier results. The update is a regulatory disclosure tied to future exploration progress, but no near-term production changes are stated. | EV Nickel Inc. reported drill-hole results from its Langmuir #2 nickel zone, including intersections from 1.07% to 5.11% Ni and higher-than-expected nickel sulphide tenor (~12% to >40% Ni). The company plans further drilling to expand the mineralized envelope and advance its nickel strategy toward battery supply. | Mink Ventures reported positive results from a summer sampling program at its Warren nickel-copper-cobalt project in Ontario. Reference samples confirmed mineralized zones with meaningful nickel grades and significant copper assays, supporting drill targeting for a planned diamond drilling phase after freeze-up. | Mink Ventures reported positive grab-sample assays from the summer sampling program at its Warren nickel-copper-cobalt project in Ontario, highlighting multiple zones with copper and nickel grades. The company plans winter access and diamond drilling after freeze-up to test refined IP/EM targets. Near-term impact on metal prices appears limited. | Fastmarkets highlights Indonesia as the core of global nickel supply and processing, with expanding intermediate and refined capacity and rising ore imports. It links this supply build-out to much lower nickel prices, citing the LME three-month contract down 47% since January 2023 and Western producer closures in a low-price environment.

Koniambo Nickel SAS said negotiations for Glencore’s 49% stake are continuing, but its furnaces were shut over the weekend and operations were already halted. Employees were laid off, and other New Caledonia nickel players are running at minimal capacity or suspending. These disruptions point to near-term nickel supply weakness. | Indonesia’s deputy minister said nickel prices should remain stable near current levels as additional capacity from new plants offsets rising EV-driven demand. He noted ore supply risks from declining nickel content and potential policy tweaks to nickel tax holidays amid the 15% global minimum corporate tax regime. Overall, near-term pressure appears mixed.

Canada Nickel said it received a Letter of Interest from Export Development Canada for up to US$500 million of long-term debt financing to support the Crawford Nickel Sulphide Project in Ontario. The funding is subject to due diligence and permits, but the milestone improves prospects for future nickel supply. | Canada Nickel said Export Development Canada is considering a loan of up to $500 million for the Crawford project in Ontario and that the company is seeking remaining funding and permits for construction. It also plans a nickel-processing plant in Ontario to process about 80,000 tonnes annually, supporting nickel supply expectations. | The piece argues global copper demand is rising from electrification and energy transition while supply faces constraints: declining ore grades, labor disruptions, fewer high-quality projects, and a large projected deficit by 2030. It details BHP’s expansion in South Australia (including refining) but says uncertainty remains about fully closing the gap. | China has filed a WTO complaint against Canada over Canada’s new tariffs on Chinese electric vehicles and 25% tariffs on steel and aluminum, with Canada’s measures set to start next month. Analysts warn retaliation could raise costs and pressure markets, adding near-term uncertainty for aluminum supply and prices. | Canada Nickel said it received an LOI for up to US$500M in financing support from Export Development Canada to fund its Crawford nickel-sulphide project, alongside expectations of qualifying for additional critical minerals and carbon capture tax credits. The company aims to secure remaining project funding and begin construction after permits.

Terrain Minerals says a recent airborne VTEM survey identified five bedrock sulphide conductors, including two within its “eye” target in a mafic/ultramafic intrusive setting, a geological style associated with nickel/copper deposits. It frames this as underexplored and potentially economic, though no drilling results are yet provided. | Falcon Gold mobilizes drill crews to begin an inaugural drilling campaign at its Great Burnt Copper Project in Newfoundland after prior access delays. The release cites high-grade prior copper drilling, outlines testing of geophysical targets, and notes ongoing permitting for additional drilling. | Falcon Gold says drill crews and equipment are being mobilized to its Great Burnt Copper Project in Newfoundland after spring access delays from high water. The firm completed 3 km of road. The property hosts reported copper-rich zones, with prior results from neighboring drilling indicating strong copper grades. | Anglo American has hired financial advisers to sell two nickel mines in Brazil (Barro Alto and Niquelandia in Goiás), producing about 38,000 tons of nickel annually. The country head says discussions with potential buyers are ongoing but no deal is expected this year, implying limited immediate supply-market disruption.
